races.race_name: Sodexo Live! 2yo Series EBF Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Hamilton Park 2yo Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Angels Passing
runners.spotlight: 18-1 and slowly away before keeping on well for fourth of ten on debut in a Listed race at York (5f, good) three weeks ago; this 130,000euros yearling could have plenty more to offer tackling 6f; strong claims.
runners.comment: Eyecatching fourth on debut in Listed race at York and she could have plenty more to offer

runners.name: Little Lady Karen
runners.spotlight: 52,000gns foal, £68,000 yearling; first foal; dam twice-raced half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Accidental Agent, out of 5f 2yo/7f winner (RPR 90); this Dark Angel newcomer takes on rivals who have shown plenty of promise but she's from a top northern stable and could have a big say.
runners.comment: £68,000 yearling; from a top northern stable and could have a big say on debut

runners.name: Meennaa
runners.spotlight: 225,000gns yearling whose dam was a very talented 2yo; backed into 4-1 when clear second on last month's debut at Kempton (6f, AW); there have been mixed messages from that form but it was a promising start to her career and she's travelled from Lambourn for this second start; has to be respected.
runners.comment: Clear second on last month's debut at Kempton; this Lambourn raider has to be respected

runners.name: Belsito
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row over C&D last September (good to soft/soft) and returned to form with very close fourth at Southwell (6f, AW) in April on his second start of the season; slow ground would be a major boost to his claims.
runners.comment: Won two in a row over C&D last September; slow ground would be a major boost to his claims

runners.name: Carlton And Co
runners.spotlight: Down the field on last month's reappearance at Catterick but may have needed that run; won three times in the second half of last year (6f/7f, good/AW) and is not discounted; has run well on good to soft going.
runners.comment: Down the field on reappearance but may have needed it; three wins in second half of 2025

runners.name: Iris Dancer
runners.spotlight: Her form has been mixed this season and she was soundly beaten at Carlisle last time; however, she's running at Hamilton for the first time this year and nine of her 11 wins have come over C&D; she's 10lb lower than when winning here last August and could bounce back with a bold show.
runners.comment: Her form has been mixed this season but she's a well handicapped course specialist

runners.name: Wish This
runners.spotlight: Won in first-time blinkers at Newcastle (6f, AW) last November but he's been well below that level in cheekpieces/blinkers on AW/turf on his three outings this spring.
runners.comment: Won at Newcastle (6f, AW) last November but well below that level this spring

runners.name: Thunderstorm Katie
runners.spotlight: Two 5f wins last October (good/AW) and runner-up over that trip on last two starts (good/good to firm), finishing well each time and shaping as though this return to 6f is well worth exploring; could play a leading role if conditions suit; best turf efforts have come on good/good to firm.
runners.comment: Has kept on well over 5f on last two starts and the return to 6f is well worth exploring

runners.name: Kelpie Grey
runners.spotlight: His losing sequence goes back 16 races to May 2024 and his form has gone the wrong way; however, he's dropped to a dangerous mark and ran respectably when fifth of nine over 7f at Ayr a fortnight ago (11-8 favourite), when shaping as though the return to 6f may help; not ruled out.
runners.comment: His losing sequence continues but on a dangerous mark and he ran respectably last time

runners.name: Invincible Ruby
runners.spotlight: Won at Catterick (7f, good to firm) last July when 6lb higher; a market check is advised but she's struggled so far this season.
runners.comment: Won last July when 6lb higher but she's struggled so far this season

runners.name: Pickersgill
runners.spotlight: Beaten a neck at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in March on third stable start then went one better at Southwell (7f, AW); won on turf at the Curragh (6f, soft) last March and this stiff 6f could be a good fit; she's one to consider, especially if slow ground prevails.
runners.comment: AW win in March on fourth yard start; one to consider, especially if slow ground prevails

runners.name: Impressor
runners.spotlight: Underwhelming on his three starts since promising reappearance run at Doncaster in March; however, he has a strong record at Hamilton and is running here for the first time this season; slow ground would be a positive.
runners.comment: Underwhelming since promising reappearance run but he has a strong record at Hamilton

runners.name: Pearl Eye
runners.spotlight: Awarded the Spring Mile at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) last March and he's fully 12lb lower today; ran well in defeat at Ayr last month on first two starts for this yard (1m/1m1f, good to firm) and he's on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Ran well in defeat at Ayr last month on first two starts for yard and is on the shortlist

runners.name: Tee Aitch Aye
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D (soft) last September when 1lb higher and he's one to watch in the betting, especially if the ground is on the slow side; however, he's been tailed off on both starts this season.
runners.comment: C&D winner who is one to watch in the betting but tailed off on both starts this season

runners.name: Penelope's Sister
runners.spotlight: Won here in 2024 and added three wins at Ayr last year; she's below her last winning mark but has been well beaten on both outings this spring and a major turnaround is necessary.
runners.comment: She's below her last winning mark but has been well beaten on both outings this spring

runners.name: Draupnir
runners.spotlight: Won at Thirsk (7f, soft) last September and reappeared with promising third there (7f, good) in April; kept on for fifth over this C&D (good) three weeks ago in a race that favoured those ridden more prominently, and the first-time hood may not have suited (left off today); possible player.
runners.comment: Reappeared with promising third at Thirsk and he's better than he showed here last time

runners.name: Obito
runners.spotlight: Always towards the rear on reappearance over C&D (good) three weeks ago but may have needed that run; won twice last summer (1m/1m2f, good; including here) and he's on a competitive mark on last autumn's form (including on soft ground); he can be in the mix if bouncing back.
runners.comment: Two wins last season (including here) and he may have needed his reappearance outing

runners.name: Highland Olly
runners.spotlight: Made all twice in a row in April (8.3f/1m, good); down the field at York (7f, good) but that was in a big field and he raced wide; could bounce back with a bold show if conditions suit (well beaten on soft going last November but displayed signs of promise on such ground early in career).
runners.comment: Made all twice in a row in April; down the field at York since but could bounce back

runners.name: Qitaal
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent and without a win since September 2024; however, he was denied a clear run when second of six at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm) recently and can be in the shake-up if backing that up; versatile ground-wise.
runners.comment: Without a win since September 2024 but denied a clear run when 2nd at Leicester recently

runners.name: Mr Lincoln
runners.spotlight: Twice placed over hurdles this spring (2m1f, good to soft/good), with a heavy defeat on the Flat at Musselburgh (1m6f, soft) in between; however, it's possible this shorter trip will be a better fit for him in this code.
runners.comment: Twice placed over hurdles this spring and today's trip may be a good fit in this code

runners.name: Eternal Force
runners.spotlight: Made it 3-3 since being gelded (good to firm/good to soft) when winning by 3l at Haydock (1m) last October; reappeared with respectable seventh of 21 in the Lincoln at Doncaster (1m, good to soft) and this lightly raced 4yo could build on that latest effort today; firmly in calculations.
runners.comment: Won final three starts last season and could build on respectable comeback run in Lincoln

runners.name: Theoryofeverything
runners.spotlight: Ran well when fifth of 18 at York (1m, good) three weeks ago on his second start of the season; his four wins (one here) have come on soft going so slower ground today would aid his cause; could be a contender.
runners.comment: Ran well when fifth of 18 at York last month and slow ground would aid this course winner

runners.name: Altareq
runners.spotlight: Went close in Newbury maiden (1m2f, good) last May on third and final outing for Gosdens and, having been detached early, he was a very promising third of 15 on his stable/handicap debut at York (1m2f, good) three weeks ago; well bred and could take a sizeable step forward from that latest effort.
runners.comment: Very lightly raced 4yo; promising third on stable debut at York & could take step forward

runners.name: Diego Ventura
runners.spotlight: He's tended to ply his trade in Group and Listed races and he was a Listed winner at Longchamp (7f, good to soft) last May; beaten just under 4l when seventh of 18 at York (1m, good) three weeks ago on his first handicap start and this is less competitive; could be in the mix.
runners.comment: Not beaten far when seventh of 18 in handicap at York and this is less competitive

runners.name: Al Mubhir
runners.spotlight: Listed win at Sandown in July 2024 and well treated on that form; however, he drew a blank during his 2025 Australian campaign and was soundly beaten on last month's stable debut in the Thirsk Hunt Cup; something to prove.
runners.comment: Well treated on old form but soundly beaten on stable debut following a stint in Australia

runners.name: Harb
runners.spotlight: Hit and miss but it was career win number nine when narrowly scoring at Carlisle 17 days ago (5f, good), coming from off the pace; 4lb higher here.
runners.comment: Hit and miss but career win number nine when narrowly scoring at Carlisle 17 days ago

runners.name: Lion's House
runners.spotlight: Has only won on Tapeta and spends a lot of his time at Newcastle; finished well behind in his last two races on turf.
runners.comment: Tapeta is his surface and finished well behind in his last two races on turf

runners.name: Water Of Leith
runners.spotlight: Won at Wolverhampton (6f, Class 5; 6-4 favourite) in March but below that level in three subsequent starts, 2l fifth of eight at Musselburgh (5f; 15-2) latest; vulnerable on those performances.
runners.comment: AW winner in March but below that level in three subsequent starts
runners.owner: Adam Mills And Jonnie Delta Racing

runners.name: Hi Lord
runners.spotlight: Headgear removed and doubled his tally with a narrow win over C&D (good; 3lb lower) a month ago; consistency hasn't always been his forte but clearly a possible contender.
runners.comment: Headgear removed and doubled his tally with a narrow win over C&D a month ago

runners.name: Call To Action
runners.spotlight: Ended 2025 with a win and better than ever when a close second of eight at Pontefract (5f, good) on reappearance; Thirsk was disappointing but that was a Class 4, so this is easier.
runners.comment: Thirsk was disappointing but that was a Class 4, so this is easier
runners.owner: D Scott, Resdev, Capla Dev & L Westwood

runners.name: Parisiac
runners.spotlight: Multiple winner who lacks a recent run but he's consistent in the main and not handicapped out of it; market could be informative.
runners.comment: Lacks a recent run but he's consistent in the main and not handicapped out of it

runners.name: Sure And Stedfast
runners.spotlight: Returned to form when third here (1m3f, good) last month on second run of the season; also effective on soft ground; could play a leading role if seeing out this longer trip, and there can be optimism on that score.
runners.comment: 3rd over 1m3f here last month and could play a leading role if seeing out this longer trip

runners.name: Perseus Way
runners.spotlight: Won over C&D (good to soft) last September and he's just 1lb higher today; won over hurdles at Hexham (2m4f, soft) last month on his second start for this yard and there's lots to like about his claims.
runners.comment: C&D winner who won over hurdles last month and there's lots to like about his claims
runners.owner: J Monkhouse R De Robeck A Miller

runners.name: Glasses Up
runners.spotlight: 11yo who was a close third over 1m1f at Ayr two starts ago and is versatile trip-wise; however, Ayr brings out the best in him and he was well beaten at Musselburgh last time; 0-4 here, with last visit coming last June.
runners.comment: Close third at Ayr two starts ago but that course brings out his best; well beaten since

runners.name: Annandale
runners.spotlight: Five-time turf winner who went close at Wolverhampton (1m6f, AW) in February when last seen; not discounted but it's possible this 8yo will improve for this first run since; question mark about slow ground nowadays.
runners.comment: Went close on AW in February when last seen but he may improve for this first run since

runners.name: Gallus Norman
runners.spotlight: Improved form when runner-up on handicap debut at Musselburgh (1m4f, good) in April and easily made all back there (2m, good) a fortnight ago; he enjoyed the run of the race last time but a 6lb rise isn't harsh in view of this unexposed 7yo's dominance; has to be respected.
runners.comment: Easily made all at Musselburgh recently and this unexposed 7yo has to be respected

runners.name: Jujubella
runners.spotlight: Well beaten last month on her two runs this year (1m2f/1m1f) and arrives with something to prove, but she's 4lb lower than when winning at Haydock (11.6f, good) last July and the step up in trip may be a positive; stable has won the last two runnings of this race with Two Auld Pals; not written off.
runners.comment: Revival required but well handicapped and stable has won last two runnings of this race

runners.name: Kisiyra
runners.spotlight: Ex-Irish 5yo who is 0-8; not beaten far when fourth of six at 66-1 at Thirsk (1m4f, good) last June on her stable debut and sole outing of the season, but she's back from another absence today and it may be best to look elsewhere; wears a first-time tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Ran pretty well on sole outing of last season but returns from another absence today

races.race_name: Hampton By Hilton Hamilton Park Handicap

runners.name: Uncle Liam
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden who arrives with plenty to prove regarding current form and stamina (untested at trip); however, his pedigree provides optimism regarding him staying and he could stage a revival when back on slow ground.
runners.comment: Lots to prove regarding current form/stamina; could stage revival when back on slow ground

runners.name: Dandy's Angel
runners.spotlight: Beverley specialist who hasn't been at the top of her game over 8.4f there on her two starts this season; however, that trip is probably inadequate nowadays and she has a reduced mark to work with back up in trip; has won over C&D and this 9yo is not written off.
runners.comment: 9yo for whom a return to form is needed but the step back up in trip could help

runners.name: Recobella
runners.spotlight: Just one win from her 17 starts but she's run really well in defeat at Doncaster on both turf outings this season (1m2f/1m4f, good to soft/good) and there's every chance she'll be bang there once more.
runners.comment: Modest strike-rate but running well in defeat on turf this year; could be bang there

runners.name: Sophiesticate
runners.spotlight: Below par on final two starts last year and same story on recent reappearance over 1m at Ayr; however, she's back up to a more suitable trip today and is 6lb below her last winning mark; not written off.
runners.comment: Return to form needed but below last winning mark and back up to a more suitable trip

runners.name: Hijo De La Luna
runners.spotlight: Won a classified race at Wolverhampton (9.4f, AW) in February in a first-time visor; however, down the field at Lingfield (1m2f, AW) in March and well beaten back over hurdles in April; needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Won on AW in February but hasn't shone since (AW/hurdles); needs to get back on track

runners.name: Ravenswell
runners.spotlight: He will have something to prove if slow ground prevails and he was tailed off on his recent reappearance over 2m at Musselburgh; however, that was his first run beyond 1m3f and he went close over that trip at Carlisle last August, when 2lb higher; might not be a forlorn hope back down in trip.
runners.comment: Tailed off on reappearance but over 2m; might not be a forlorn hope back down in trip

runners.name: Sugarpiehoneybunch
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten on final three starts last season and same story on reappearance in April; however, she might have needed that latest outing and she won this last year; shouldn't be discounted.
runners.comment: No return to form on reappearance but may have needed it and she won this last year

runners.name: Falaise Blanc
runners.spotlight: Won at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) last August and runner-up at Musselburgh (1m4f, good to soft) next time; however, he's been well below that level so far this season and a major turnaround is necessary.
runners.comment: Of interest on his form last August/September but well below that level so far this season

runners.name: Wath Court
runners.spotlight: Longstanding maiden who has been well beaten on all four starts this year and would be a surprise winner today.
runners.comment: Longstanding maiden who has been well beaten on all four starts this year

runners.name: Storm On Jura
runners.spotlight: 4yo who has raced only six times but has been well beaten in her three handicaps, the latest her reappearance at Musselburgh in April.
runners.comment: 4yo who has raced only six times but has been well beaten in her three handicaps

runners.name: Ivynator
runners.spotlight: Runner-up over hurdles last May but this 8yo has struggled to get competitive on his four starts since, one of those being on the Flat; races for the first time since last September and others make greater appeal.
runners.comment: 2nd over hurdles last May but hasn't got competitive on his four starts since (one Flat)

races.race_name: Vauxhall Knaresborough Britsh EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (B/C/D) (Qual') (GBB) (Div I)

runners.name: Ziggy Starshine
runners.spotlight: Well-backed debut winner at Bath (5f, good) in April, when appearing to have plenty in hand; also supported at Salisbury (5f, good to firm) last time but she didn't manage to step up on that first effort and found one too good; sets the standard though and is a likely player again.
runners.comment: Debut winner (5f, good); unable to better that on latest but still sets the standard.

runners.name: Eleven Eighty Two
runners.spotlight: 12,000euros yearling; fourth foal; half-sister to French 6f 2yo winner Charismatic Chick; dam placed 7.5f in France, out of maiden half-sister to 5f Group 1 winner Gilt Edge Girl and 5f 2yo Group 2 winner Godfrey Street; trainer not known for 2yo turf winners.
runners.comment: Half-sister to French 6f 2yo winner Charismatic Chick; trainer not known for 2yo winners.

runners.name: Ma Fille De Reve
runners.spotlight: Fourth foal; half-sister to 6f/8.5f winner Yiengaa (including Germany; RPR 58); dam 6f/7f winner (including AW 2yo; 101), half-sister to useful 1m 2yo winner Madeed; trainer 7-115 with 2yos on turf in recent seasons but has had one go in on the AW this year.
runners.comment: Dam 6f/7f winner; trainer 7-115 with 2yos on turf in the last five years

runners.name: Hoosier
runners.spotlight: 48,000euros yearling; second foal; half-sister to 7f AW winner Royal County Glory (RPR 63); dam 1m AW winner (62), closely related to 1m winner Sienna Lady, half-sister to 7f Group 2 winner Shakespearean; trainer 12% with turf 2yos the last five seasons; market check suggested.
runners.comment: 48,000euros yearling; trainer 12% with turf 2yos the last five seasons; check the market

runners.name: Wateera
runners.spotlight: 52,000euros yearling; fourth foal; half-sister to 5f AW 2yo winner Sands Of Josepi (RPR 76); dam French 6f 2yo winner, half-sister to French winners Millau (useful 6f/7f) and Emblematique (6f/7.5f 2yo); trainer 21% with 2yos on turf over the last five seasons; check the market for clues.
runners.comment: 52,000euros yearling; trainer 21% with turf 2yos over the last five seasons; check betting

runners.name: Classic Bellini
runners.spotlight: 28-1 on debut at Thirsk (5f, good) last month, when slow to get going and never better than midfield; half-sister to a Listed sprint-winning 2yo and no surprise if she is able to step up on that.
runners.comment: Half-sister to a Listed 2yo winner; no surprise if she is able to step up on her debut.

races.race_name: Vauxhall Knaresborough Britsh EBF Fillies' Restricted Novice Stakes (B/C/D) (Qual') (GBB) (Div II)

runners.name: Cheeky Chesca
runners.spotlight: Has improved with each start and was collared late on her most recent start at Redcar (6f, good to firm); should be competitive and the drop back to 5f may help; each-way player.
runners.comment: Collared late at Redcar latest (6f); should be competitive back in trip; each-way player.

runners.name: Lairy Mary
runners.spotlight: Built on some minor promise when breaking her duck in a Beverley claimer (5f, good to firm) last week, when racing prominently; will probably need more to win this but looks an each-way player.
runners.comment: Won a Beverley claimer (5f, good to firm) last week; each-way player.

runners.name: Someone To Love
runners.spotlight: 25,000euros yearling; fourth foal; dam 1m/8.5f AW winner (including 2yo/US stakes), half-sister to Canadian 1m2f Grade 1 winner Blond Me; trainer having a good season with 2yos on turf, 7-24, so the betting needs a check for clues.
runners.comment: Trainer 7-24 with 2yos on turf this season so the betting needs a check for clues

runners.name: Tzaritza
runners.spotlight: £38,000 2yo; third foal; half-sister to 6f 2yo winner Rich Glory (RPR 79); dam placed twice at 7f in France, half-sister to winners Second City (Canadian 1m1f Grade 3) and Lunch Hour (US 7.5f); trainer 5-23 with 2yos on turf this year; interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: Trainer 5-23 with 2yos on turf this season; interesting newcomer

runners.name: Angel Steps
runners.spotlight: 3,000euros yearling; fifth foal; half-sister to winners Indigo Dawn (6f 2yo; RPR 74), Jaadil (2m2f hurdle), Making Magic (Italian 7.5f) and Scatola Chiusa (Italian 1m-1m4f including 2yo); dam maiden (44) half-sister to Group-placed 6f 2yo winner Unilit; trainer not known for 2yo winners.
runners.comment: Half-sister to four winners; dam maiden; trainer not known for 2yo turf winners.

runners.name: Isabella Blue
runners.spotlight: 25,000euros yearling; third foal; dam unraced half-sister to Canadian 1m 2yo Grade 1 winner Capla Temptress, out of 1m winning half-sister to 1m2f Group 3 winner Stotsfold; trainer combination 9% with turf 2yos; market check advised.
runners.comment: 25,000euros yearling who is worth a market check on debut

runners.name: Heffernan Kingdom
runners.spotlight: Easy to back on debut when showing minor promise in finishing under 4l sixth at Thirsk (5f, good) last month; could be a player if she can step up on that.
runners.comment: Minor promise on debut at Thirsk (5f, good); possible player if stepping up on that.

runners.name: Beautiful Rainbow
runners.spotlight: Moderate form at Beverley and Ascot (both 5f, good); likely to need a significant step up on that and will make greater appeal once she has a handicap mark.
runners.comment: Moderate form in two starts and will make more appeal in nurseries.

runners.name: Mardy Bum
runners.spotlight: 9-1 for both starts but has finished last each time, her debut effort (5f, AW) being better than her more recent turf start (5f, good to firm); much more needed for the first-time hood.
runners.comment: Has finished last on both starts (9-1 each time); much more needed for new hood.

runners.name: Overbudget
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise when second on her first two starts (both 5f, good to soft) last season; not as good on her reappearance at Wolverhampton (5f, AW) or on handicap debut at Windsor (5f, good to firm) latest; each-way player if back to her best.
runners.comment: Showed promise as a juvenile but has gone backwards; each-way player if back to her best.
runners.owner: Matt Broughton & Hold My Beer Syndicate

runners.name: Reigning Queen
runners.spotlight: Has shown some promise in two starts (5f, AW; 6f, soft), finishing second at Yarmouth last time; could appreciate this slightly shorter trip and is respected from a top yard.
runners.comment: Has shown promise; could appreciate this slightly shorter trip and is respected.

runners.name: Viviana
runners.spotlight: Showed promise when sticking on for third on debut (6f, good to firm) at Redcar; not as good on soft ground at Yarmouth latest but could be involved if bouncing back.
runners.comment: Promising debut but not as good latest; could be involved if bouncing back.

runners.name: Slot
runners.spotlight: Has shown promise on several occasions, matching her best form on handicap debut at Doncaster (6f, good to soft) latest; more needed to win but looks an each-way player.
runners.comment: Matched her best form on handicap debut latest; more needed to win but an each-way player.

runners.name: Veil Of Clouds
runners.spotlight: Second foal; half-sister to 5f winner Mon Na Slieve (including 2yo; RPR 96); dam 7f/1m winner (including AW; 109), sister to Group-placed 6f 2yo/1m2f winner Artigiano; interesting pedigree and no surprise if she acquits herself well for leading yard.
runners.comment: Interesting pedigree and no surprise if she goes well for her leading yard on debut

runners.name: Black Orchid
runners.spotlight: 7-4 on debut at Redcar (6f, good) last month when not getting the smoothest of passages but kept on for second behind a 7l winner; can probably step up on that.
runners.comment: 7l second on debut at Redcar when not getting a clear run; can probably step up on that.

runners.name: Nanoscience
runners.spotlight: Delivered on 2yo promise when winning Kempton maiden (7f, AW) on seasonal debut in April and was placed in quite valuable Newmarket handicap (7f, good to firm) last month; that latest piece of form reads well and she's a big player from the inside stall here.
runners.comment: Placed in quite valuable Newmarket contest on last month's handicap debut; big player

runners.name: Magic Box
runners.spotlight: Dual 7f nursery winner from eight 2yo starts; might have more to offer this year but made underwhelming reappearance last month and has wide draw.
runners.comment: Dual nursery winner; made underwhelming reappearance last month and has wide draw

runners.name: Seeing Stars
runners.spotlight: Runner-up three consecutive times before winning 7f Kempton (AW) maiden in January, and again ran well when placed on last month's handicap debut at Wolverhampton (7f, AW); could still have more to offer but is not drawn well for this turf debut.
runners.comment: Has made very solid start to her career on AW but is not drawn well for this turf debut

runners.name: Dream Illusion
runners.spotlight: Gained all four wins on Tapeta, the last three over 1m this year; well down the field when back on turf last month, though, and this drop back to 7f may not be ideal.
runners.comment: Three 1m AW wins this year; well down the field when back on turf last month, though

runners.name: Lily Pearl
runners.spotlight: Ended four-race 2yo campaign with win on nursery debut at Pontefract (6f, good to soft) and recovered from awkward start to finish third on last month's reappearance at Catterick (6f, good); upped in trip; shortlisted.
runners.comment: Recovered well from awkward start to finish third on seasonal debut; upped from 6f today

runners.name: Volendam
runners.spotlight: Belatedly opened account with clear win at Bath (1m, good) in April, her 18th start, and rebounded from lesser effort when second at Nottingham (8.3f, good to soft) last month; has a tendency to race too freely, so this drop back in trip could be a good move; others have more potential, though.
runners.comment: Belatedly off the mark in April but others in this line-up probably have more potential

runners.name: Borjina
runners.spotlight: Unexposed after only two starts and justified heavy market support when readily winning Catterick novice (7f, good) on seasonal debut in April; judged on the subsequent exploits of the runner-up in handicaps, this filly could be on a lenient opening mark.
runners.comment: Readily won Catterick novice in April and could be on a lenient opening mark
runners.owner: All About York And Whatton Manor Stud

runners.name: Come On Eibhlin
runners.spotlight: Posted sound effort when third at Windsor (1m, good to firm) 12 days ago and may benefit from this drop back in trip but she's not making any progress and others have more appealing profiles.
runners.comment: Posted sound effort when third last month but is not making any progress
runners.owner: Hold My Beer, Gee Gees Racing & N Clark

runners.name: Albeseeingyer
runners.spotlight: Left low-key seasonal debut behind when second at Leicester (7f, good; well backed) in April; remains on a good mark but was safely held when back at Leicester last month and is drawn wide here.
runners.comment: Still on good mark after Leicester second two starts ago but drawn wide here

runners.name: Folkene
runners.spotlight: Ex-French filly who won on last summer's British debut at Newmarket (7f, good to firm); launched this campaign with two perfectly respectable runs, the latter over C&D, but others have more pressing claims all the same.
runners.comment: Both runs this season were perfectly respectable but others have more pressing claims

races.race_name: Malvern Castle And Compass Hospitality Handicap

runners.name: Ciao Capo
runners.spotlight: 1m AW winner; first turf win when upped to 1m2f at Beverley (good to firm) in May; 3lb rise suggests he is still well treated; big player.
runners.comment: Won going away at Beverley (1m2f, good to firm) latest; up 3lb; big player.

runners.name: Stepanov
runners.spotlight: Peak effort came when second on his second start at Wolverhampton (9.5f, AW) in March; has not proved as effective since, including on handicap debut at Nottingham (1m2f, good to soft) latest; drops 2lb and is on a competitive mark if able to return to his best.
runners.comment: Placed on AW; drops 2lb and is on a competitive mark if able to return to his best.

runners.name: Skirt Around
runners.spotlight: Showed a degree of promise in a fillies' novice event at Newcastle on her third start (7f, AW); may have needed last month's handicap debut after a break, when upped to 1m2f at the same track (nibbled at in the market); drops 3lb; Oisin Murphy booked; check the market for further clues.
runners.comment: May have needed reappearance; drops 3lb; O Murphy booked; check the market

runners.name: Dalamara
runners.spotlight: 1m AW winner last December when 4lb lower; has bettered that effort in defeat on both subsequent starts on turf; step up in trip could help tease out a bit of improvement; each-way player.
runners.comment: 1m AW winner; step up in trip could help tease out a bit of improvement; each-way player.

runners.name: Sovereign Bright
runners.spotlight: 0-9; best effort came as a juvenile when a close second at Doncaster (7f, good to firm; RPR 70); placed four times since and is slipping back to a more competitive mark; well below par latest but each-way player on best efforts.
runners.comment: Placed in 5-9 starts; well below par latest but each-way player if back to best

runners.name: India Gold
runners.spotlight: Off the mark with a 7f soft-ground success (first-time hood) at Musselburgh last November; 10lb rise hasn't helped her in two subsequent starts; more needed.
runners.comment: 7f winner (soft) but 10lb rise appears to have anchored her since; more needed.

runners.name: Rogue Desire
runners.spotlight: Showed some promise on debut when third at Salisbury (7f, good to firm) but took a couple of backward steps since then; tongue-tie fitted for stable debut; perhaps best watched.
runners.comment: Third (7f) on debut but gone backwards since; perhaps best watched on stable debut.

runners.name: Riddikulus
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in six starts and only moderate form; others preferred.
runners.comment: Unplaced in six starts and only moderate form; others preferred.

runners.name: Haltonwood
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in four starts but minor promise when beaten under 3l on his handicap debut at Doncaster (1m, good to soft; first-time tongue-tie) latest; up 1lb; step up in trip looks a positive and he wouldn't need a huge amount of improvement to get involved; each-way player.
runners.comment: Took step in right direction latest; step up in trip is a positive; each-way player.

races.race_name: Book Your Autumn Hospitality Packages Now Handicap

runners.name: Inspired
runners.spotlight: 1m AW winner last December, his peak effort; not far off that level at York latest (7f, good) after being gelded and having a break; drops 1lb; interesting.
runners.comment: Not far off his best at York latest (7f, good) after a break; drops 1lb; interesting.
runners.owner: Fine Claret Racing,Mrs Burke & C Wood

runners.name: Yaajooz
runners.spotlight: Won a 1m selling handicap (good to firm) at Thirsk when with George Boughey; has gone close a couple of times for this yard, including here (1m, good) 12 months ago when 3lb higher; below par in both starts this season but not written off.
runners.comment: 1m winner; went close here (1m) last year; below par this season but not written off.

runners.name: Livonian
runners.spotlight: Odds-on 1m winner (good) at Windsor last September when with Ralph Beckett; has gone backwards in all three starts since, latest on debut for new yard; tongue-tie now tried; plenty to prove at present.
runners.comment: Has gone backwards since 1m win (good); tongue-tie now tried; plenty to prove at present.

runners.name: Anthropologist
runners.spotlight: 7f-1m AW winner and 7f turf winner (good) last year when 6lb lower; well below par on both AW and turf on his last two starts and he does have a little to prove after that.
runners.comment: Well below par on last two outings and has a little to prove after that.

runners.name: Symphony Of Joy
runners.spotlight: Best effort from four starts was on debut (7f, good); beaten 9l in a Windsor maiden handicap (1m, good to firm) when last seen; has since had wind surgery; probably best watched unless market vibes are strong.
runners.comment: Best effort was on debut; well beaten latest; had wind surgery since; best watched.

runners.name: Catalyse
runners.spotlight: Debut 6f winner (good) who has been unable to better that effort or add to that success in 11 further starts; well beaten twice since switching to his current trainer but it's not long since this would have looked a very handy mark and he would be a big player if bouncing back.
runners.comment: 6f winner (good); below par lately but handy mark if able to bounce back.

runners.name: Empirestateofmind
runners.spotlight: Prolific winner at 1m-1m1f on good or slower; inconsistent this season but has slipped to a workable mark and, if in the same form as at Ripon on penultimate start, he could have a say.
runners.comment: Slipped to a workable mark and, if in the form of his penultimate start, could have a say.

runners.name: War Howl
runners.spotlight: Won back-to-back 7f races on sound surfaces last summer when with Mick Appleby but has been well below that level of form in all five starts for his current trainer; very handy mark but no recent signs of revival.
runners.comment: 7f winner, but well below his best for this trainer; handy mark but no sign of revival.
runners.owner: Future Champions Racing Syn & Ben Haslam

runners.name: Stratocracy
runners.spotlight: Two-time turf and AW winner (7f-1m); 8lb higher than his highest winning mark and although he may have needed his reappearance, he may also now need a favour from the handicapper.
runners.comment: Two-time turf and AW winner (7f-1m) who may need a favour from the handicapper.
runners.owner: Total Asbestos Solutions Ltd & A Frost

runners.name: Lady Mariko
runners.spotlight: Two-time turf winner over 6f-7f; career best came with an AW win at Kempton in April; well beaten in two 7f turf starts since; drops 1lb; more needed.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two turf starts since career best on AW; drops 1lb; more needed.

runners.name: Madame De Sevigne
runners.spotlight: Twice a 7f winner, including from this mark, in 2024; recent AW form has been significantly better than that on turf; cheekpieces now tried but she has a bit to prove on this surface.
runners.comment: 7f winner; recent form better on AW than turf; cheekpieces now but she has a bit to prove.

runners.name: It Just Takes Time
runners.spotlight: Prolific winner at up to 7.5f, on all types of ground; 3lb lower than when last scoring at Beverley last September and, with his trainer among the winners, don't be surprised if he returns to form.
runners.comment: Prolific winner; handy mark and from in-form yard, so he may be able to return to form.

runners.name: Obelix
runners.spotlight: 7f and 1m winner on good; has been unable to return to the heights of his most recent win at York last July, in six subsequent starts; back on a handy mark; could be better for his reappearance and is not written off.
runners.comment: 7f/1m winner; handy mark; could be better for his reappearance and is not written off.
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd & Julie Camacho

runners.name: Orne
runners.spotlight: 7f turf (heavy) and AW winner but without success since 2023; inconsistent but his better efforts of the last 12 months would give him each-way claims from this mark.
runners.comment: Inconsistent but his better efforts of the last year would give him each-way claims.

runners.name: Society Man
runners.spotlight: Triple slow turf winner (7f-1m) who has recently been on the slide in the handicap; takes another slight drop in grade but it's hard to be confident that it will end a barren spell.
runners.comment: Triple winner on slow turf; hard to be confident drop in grade will end this barren spell.

races.race_name: Live Streams On Racing TV Extra Handicap

runners.name: Crocus Time
runners.spotlight: Left low-key reappearance run behind when scoring at Redcar (5f, good to firm) nine days ago and is now 2-7 for Ron Barr; carries 5lb penalty here but is due to go up by that amount anyway and the slightly longer trip should be in her favour; likely contender.
runners.comment: Scored at Redcar last week and is now 2-7 for Ron Barr; likely contender again

runners.name: He's An Angel
runners.spotlight: Often blows the start and/or races too freely but is a six-time AW winner, most recently in 5f Wolverhampton classified on Monday; faces tougher task under 5lb penalty in this handicap and his turf strike-rate is 0-13.
runners.comment: Won AW classified on Monday but faces tougher task here and is 0-13 on turf

runners.name: Highfield Jewel
runners.spotlight: Still unexposed after only four runs but soundly beaten when 40-1 for recent handicap debut at Southwell (7f, AW); dropped below 6f for first time here; others have much more obvious claims.
runners.comment: Unexposed after only four runs but well beaten on recent handicap debut

runners.name: Eldeyaar
runners.spotlight: Seemingly perked up by change of headgear when winning from the front at Thirsk (6f, good; first-time visor) in April and again ran well when third of 17 over that C&D last month; high on the list.
runners.comment: Won in new headgear at Thirsk in April and ran well again in big field there last month

runners.name: Sir Benedict
runners.spotlight: No longer the force of old but has performed well on all four starts since joining Ben Haslam in March, most recently when keeping-on third at Musselburgh (5f, good) a fortnight ago; big player if this race is run to suit usual come-from-behind tactics.
runners.comment: Has performed well on all four starts for Ben Haslam and can threaten from off the pace

runners.name: South Shore Island
runners.spotlight: 24-race maiden, absent since three midfield finishes on Lingfield Polytrack (5f-6f) for Amanda Perrett in December; changed hands for 10,000gns in January; makes stable debut from workable mark.
runners.comment: 24-race maiden; absent since December but makes stable debut from a workable mark

runners.name: Amerjeet
runners.spotlight: Well handicapped on some of his winter AW form but was safely held when back on turf at Beverley (5f, good to firm) last week and remains without a win since summer 2024; others preferred.
runners.comment: Well handicapped on winter AW form but safely held when back on turf eight days ago

runners.name: Highland Bear
runners.spotlight: Displayed promise in all three qualifying races, most recently when returning from 479-day absence at Southwell (6f, AW) in April; makes turf/handicap debut in a low-grade race; interesting.
runners.comment: Showed promise in all three qualifying races; makes handicap/turf debut in low-grade race

runners.name: Dragon God
runners.spotlight: Still a maiden after ten runs but has been placed twice on good to firm ground this spring (6f/5f) and may not have been suited by good to soft when slightly disappointing last time; this trip could be ideal; no surprise if he features.
runners.comment: Placed over 6f and 5f this spring and had a possible ground excuse last time; considered

runners.name: Mrs Trump
runners.spotlight: Yet to strike form after two 5f runs this season; now 2lb lower than for the second of last summer's two 6f wins, though, and it probably won't be much longer before she gets back in the groove.
runners.comment: Yet to strike form after two runs this season but now 2lb below last winning mark
runners.owner: R Chapman, G J Daly & R J Parks

races.race_name: Book Tickets Online At wetherbyracing.co.uk Handicap

runners.name: Powernap
runners.spotlight: Took a step back in the right direction at Catterick (5f, good to firm; first-time tongue-tie) latest; each-way player if she can back that up.
runners.comment: Took a step back in the right direction latest; each-way player if backing that up.

runners.name: Data Fata Secutus
runners.spotlight: Has twice gone close from this mark in her last three starts, at Musselburgh (5f, good) and most recently at Redcar (5f, good to firm); both those efforts suggest that this extra half furlong will be a positive; visor now tried; player.
runners.comment: Recent efforts suggest that this extra half furlong will be a positive; visored; player.

runners.name: Ellie's de Vega
runners.spotlight: Placed twice over 6f on a sound surface; below best at Ripon (6f, good) on latest start; each-way player but will need improvement to score.
runners.comment: Below her best at Ripon latest; each-way player but will need some improvement to score.

runners.name: Ryefield
runners.spotlight: Much his best effort when second in a Pontefract maiden (6f, good to soft) last September; well below that level in four subsequent starts and is plummeting in the handicap; cheekpieces need to have a positive effect.
runners.comment: Below par on his last four starts; cheekpieces need to have a positive effect.

runners.name: Northern Tempest
runners.spotlight: Peak effort came in a Newcastle novice event (5f, AW) last November; well beaten in his first two handicaps but showed more last time at Ripon (6f, good); same mark; handy draw and slightly shorter trip both look positive; could go well.
runners.comment: Better effort latest; handy draw and slightly shorter trip both positive; could go well.
runners.owner: Ursa Major Racing Ltd And M Dods

runners.name: Dabbling
runners.spotlight: Inconsistent and hasn't yet hinted that she is up to winning from this mark; more needed.
runners.comment: Inconsistent and hasn't yet hinted that she is up to winning from this mark; more needed.

runners.name: Surgeon Commander
runners.spotlight: Two-time 7f AW winner who was placed from this mark in that sphere last time; his juvenile turf form was moderate but he's not ruled out back up in trip and back on this surface for the first time in 11 months.
runners.comment: Not ruled out back up in trip and back on this surface for the first time in 11 months.

runners.name: Sea Her Excel
runners.spotlight: Peak effort when last of three in a Newcastle fillies' novice (6f, AW) last September; took a step back in the right direction at Catterick (5f, good to firm) latest; more needed.
runners.comment: Took step back in the right direction at Catterick (5f, good to firm) latest; more needed.

races.race_name: Racing To School Reaches 25 Years Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Loriko
runners.spotlight: Well below the fairly useful form he showed in the autumn when beaten odds-on favourite in Plumpton maiden (2m, good) in April but made amends when winning tactical match race at Huntingdon (2m4f, good) last month; his profile is still not entirely convincing but he has strong claims here.
runners.comment: Does not have entirely convincing profile but won a match last month; strong claims
runners.owner: Cherry Knoll Farm, M Ward & D Skelton

runners.name: Starshine Legend
runners.spotlight: Fair Flat racer who gained his win over 1m4f two years ago; back from long layoff with two underwhelming runs for new stable this spring but still has possibilities if he takes well to hurdling at the first attempt.
runners.comment: Fair Flat racer; not at best since long absence but still considered on hurdle debut

runners.name: White Riot
runners.spotlight: Won good-ground bumper by 8l in March 2025; rather disappointing when well-held third on hurdle debut in October and has failed to complete twice since but shaped with promise before weakening and falling at Southwell (2m, good) last month, after a break.
runners.comment: Bumper winner; not much has gone to plan over hurdles but he still enters calculations

runners.name: Biggles
runners.spotlight: Very useful on the Flat in his prime, with career highlight his 2023 Bunbury Cup success (7f; unraced beyond about 1m); absent since two below-par runs in spring 2024, though, and tricky to weigh up on stable/hurdle debut.
runners.comment: 2023 Bunbury Cup winner; makes stable/hurdle debut after long layoff; tricky to weigh up
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Lady Cobham

runners.name: Minella Blizzard
runners.spotlight: Out of a well-connected mare but was pulled up on his Irish point debut in April; the betting market may guide on this rules debut.
runners.comment: Out of a well-connected mare but was pulled up on his Irish point debut in April

runners.name: Coumeenoole
runners.spotlight: Miles clear with one other when beaten just over 1l in a Market Rasen 0-125 handicap latest (3m, good; RPR 118), and this novice contest isn't anywhere near as deep; yet to register form of that calibre with give underfoot, but possible he could get away with it here even if rain arrives.
runners.comment: Close second in a Market Rasen 0-125 last time out (3m, good) sets the standard.

runners.name: Presentandcorrect
runners.spotlight: 23l clear of Stavanger Rose when third at Cartmel on last month's hurdling debut (2m6f, good), beaten about 4l but quite strong in the finish; coped fine with cut in bumpers and argues a solid case.
runners.comment: Kept on into a near-4l third on Cartmel hurdles debut latest (2m6f, good); handles cut.

runners.name: Millena Agent
runners.spotlight: 2m4f Hexham maiden point winner last November (heavy), and best effort since when second in her members latest; some encouragement for staying trips on pedigree (family of Monger Lane), and won't mind any rain.
runners.comment: Only 1-11 in points, but best effort since her heavy-ground maiden win last time out.

runners.name: Executive Producer
runners.spotlight: Big uplift on previous hurdling efforts when 15l fourth over 2m4f here latest (good), though late weakening doesn't necessarily augur well for this step up in trip (didn't see out 3m in Irish points).
runners.comment: Much better effort when fourth here latest, albeit not seeing out 2m4f; up in trip.

runners.name: Up The Hill
runners.spotlight: Bred to appreciate a trip and hurdles, but must settle an awful lot better than on either bumper start last winter; gains tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Bred to need a trip and hurdles, but needs to settle better than she did in bumpers.
runners.owner: Mrs K Jeanes & Mrs P Walker

runners.name: Stavanger Rose
runners.spotlight: Accident prone during her time with Daisy Hitchins, albeit shaping with promise sometimes; failed to see out 2m6f when finally registering a completion on stable debut latest, however.
runners.comment: Has ability, but failed to see out 2m6f when finally registering completion latest.

runners.name: Secret Trix
runners.spotlight: C&D record of 31133 includes a soft-ground win off 9lb higher almost two years ago and last month's perfectly serviceable staying-on 10l third in 0-145 company; faces an easier-looking task this afternoon.
runners.comment: C&D exploits include a win off 9lb higher (soft) and last month's 0-145 third; player.

runners.name: Faded Fantasy
runners.spotlight: Three good-ground wins at up to 3m2f during a profitable 2025, all off at least 9lb lower; yet to defy a break in excess of 45 days, so absence since January cannot rate a plus.
runners.comment: Profitable 2025, but currently 9lb above his last winning mark; might just need this.

runners.name: Breezethroughlife
runners.spotlight: December's Doncaster second (3m, good to soft) suggested a sound future as a stayer, so it's disappointing he has not always seen out mostly shorter trips since, beaten 26l over almost 2m5f latest.
runners.comment: Doncaster second last December (3m), but not seen out this or shorter trips well since.

runners.name: Top Coeur
runners.spotlight: Not seeing out his races at all convincingly this year, beaten 54l in a 2m4f 0-125 handicap here latest (2m4f, good); overlooked.
runners.comment: 54l last of eight here latest (2m4f); looks the least compelling of the Williams pair.

runners.name: Mammies Boy
runners.spotlight: Despite again hanging left may have gone closer than a 2l third at Exeter latest (2m6f, good) but for whacking two out; stayers' trip asks a new question, but some appeal whilst still just below a winning mark.
runners.comment: Hung left again when beaten 2l latest but late mistake more the issue; tries new trip.

runners.name: Tiger Orchid
runners.spotlight: Four good efforts from as many starts for current connections, though once again wasn't quite able to convert a winning opportunity at Huntingdon latest (3m1f, good to firm); fair claims off an unchanged mark, and ought to cope with any rain.
runners.comment: Hasn't held on to any winning opportunity lately; chance off unchanged mark even so.

runners.name: Belvedere Blast
runners.spotlight: Five out of seven hurdles wins have been recorded in the month of June; mark and trip fine, but although once a winner after 151 days out his record following an absence is a bit mixed overall.
runners.comment: ?rip and time of year both in his favour; not absolutely certain to be at his best fresh.

races.race_name: JMI Planning 10 Years In Business Mares' Handicap Chase (ARC Summer Chase Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Queens Wish
runners.spotlight: 1-10 for current stable over the last year, the win gained off today's mark at Southwell (3m, good to soft) in November; needs to bounce back from a disappointing effort over that C&D last month but ran quite well in a valuable series final two starts ago and is of interest here.
runners.comment: Didn't fire on last outing but was in fair form beforehand and is back on her winning mark

runners.name: Regal Renaissance
runners.spotlight: 2-13 over fences when trained by Henry Daly, the latter win gained at Ludlow (2m4f, good) last month; yet fully to convince stamina-wise over 3m but, having recently changed hands for £22,000, still has strong claims on this stable debut.
runners.comment: Signed off for Henry Daly with 2m4f Ludlow win last month; strong claims on stable debut

runners.name: That's Nice
runners.spotlight: 2-3 as a novice hurdler during 2024-25 season but 0-5 and generally disappointing since switched to chasing in November; didn't run badly when third of four in higher-grade event here (2m4f, good) last month, though, and is still unexposed over this sort of trip.
runners.comment: 2-3 over hurdles; 0-5 and generally disappointing over fences but drops in grade today

runners.name: I Am The Moon
runners.spotlight: Failed to convince as a chaser in two attempts at last season; subsequently won over hurdles (2m3f, soft) but her form dipped afterwards; took step back in right direction when well-held second in four-runner hurdle last month (3m2f, good to firm) but needs another on this return to fences.
runners.comment: Failed to convince in two chasing attempts last term; ran just okay over hurdles recently

runners.name: Bells Of Ufford
runners.spotlight: Consolidated rather than improved upon previous best efforts during last season, despite the addition of cheekpieces; remains on a tempting mark, but significant rain wouldn't be welcomed.
runners.comment: Yet to fare significantly better since cheekpieces went on; won't want appreciable rain.

runners.name: The Best Way
runners.spotlight: Determined last-gasp Newcastle winner in February 2025 (2m1f, good to soft), and recent Hexham third on sole run since was much more promising than the 31l distance beaten might imply (rallied after late blunder); may be a case of whether this longer trip is within range now.
runners.comment: 31l third on comeback run was better than the bare facts; chance if staying 2m4f now.

runners.name: Saxon Cross
runners.spotlight: Last month's staying-on 5l fourth in a 2m novice here was given a boost by the third at Southwell two days ago; this is a handy initial mark set against his bumper best; kept almost exclusively to good ground so far.
runners.comment: Just behind a next-time winner when a staying-on fourth here latest (2m); useful mark.

runners.name: The Padster
runners.spotlight: Back on track to an extent with last month's fast-ground Hereford 2m3f second in debuted cheekpieces (retained), albeit beaten 21l; ground slower than good would ask a new question.
runners.comment: Second at Hereford latest (2m3f), albeit beaten 21l; untried on ground slower than good.

runners.name: Brionywells
runners.spotlight: Major personal best on last week's return from eight months out, staying on well late to take a 2m7f Worcester novice handicap (good); little to choose between her peak previous RPRs with or without cut, so she may well cope if the rain arrives; player despite the penalty.
runners.comment: Worcester 2m7f winner last week (good) following eight months out; player despite penalty.

runners.name: Hillsin
runners.spotlight: The consistency of his summer 2024 campaign has long deserted him, the return to the mark of October's Exeter win (2m2f, good to firm) owed to three poor runs since; others convince more.
runners.comment: No longer as consistent as two summers ago, though he's back on a winning mark, at least.

runners.name: Pepite De Saphir
runners.spotlight: Career already subject to two significant interruptions, and offered little in a debuted tongue-tie when starting out for current connections here last month (2m7f, good).
runners.comment: Offered little in debuted tongue-tie on stable debut here latest (off for 448 days prior).

runners.name: Bowling Shane
runners.spotlight: Better round of jumping inspired a major uplift on last month's handicap debut at Ffos Las (2m, good), shaping as if this longer trip might give him more chance of defying a still ungenerous-looking mark.
runners.comment: Jumped better when third on Ffos Las handicap debut latest; step up from 2m may suit.

races.race_name: Turf Services Handicap Chase (ARC Summer Chase Series Qualifier) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Wheresmemoneygone
runners.spotlight: Made all in debuted cheekpieces at Taunton in February (2m5f, good to soft); 7lb rise for posting that course record time was fair, and the subsequent Newbury defeat instead looked owed to the same sort of fight for the early lead which Joltin N Jiving might yet give him here.
runners.comment: Broke 2m5f course record when making all at Taunton; faces possible pace rivalry here.

runners.name: Yes Indeed
runners.spotlight: Respectable efforts on both previous course visits, including a close fourth in this in 2024; weighted to strike, but must prove that the effects of the cheekpieces aren't on the wane after a sub-par latest effort.
runners.comment: Close fourth in this two years ago; weighted to strike, but ran below par last time.

runners.name: Immortal Fame
runners.spotlight: 2m4f winner here over hurdles and fences with a little give underfoot, and possible excuses on both starts since his most recent success elsewhere (holding ground, pace battle); remains above any winning mark.
runners.comment: 2m4f winner here over both hurdles and fences; possible excuses on his last two starts.

runners.name: Joltin N Jiving
runners.spotlight: Resumed previous progression with last time's Doncaster handicap hurdle second (2m5f, good to soft) off 1lb lower; switched to front-running there, and would likely face competition for the lead if that strategy is persisted with today; retains tongue-tie for chasing debut.
runners.comment: Back on track with last time's Doncaster hurdles second (2m5f); tongue-tie retained.
runners.owner: N J Clayton, Matthew Reddy & Jude Mallon

runners.name: Karnaval Point
runners.spotlight: Not hard pushed to score on last month's stable/British debut in a 2m4f Worcester handicap chase (good), for which a 9lb rise isn't harsh; French chase winner at up to 2m7f on slow ground, so appears to have an answer for most eventualities; player.
runners.comment: Up 9lb for cosy British/stable debut win (2m4f, good); won't mind longer trip or any rain.

runners.name: Ask A Sainte
runners.spotlight: Unexposed 6yo who kept on from the rear for encouraging fourth behind Genbu over C&D (good) on last month's stable/handicap debut; probably still has a bigger run in him and features prominently in calculations.
runners.comment: Encouraging handicap debut when fourth behind Genbu here last month; can improve

runners.name: Zucayan
runners.spotlight: Gained all three hurdle wins at Ludlow; kept on for second behind progressive rival there (2m, good) last month, after a break, but may not be ideally suited by this switch to a left-handed track.
runners.comment: Ran well at favourite track Ludlow last month, after a break; may not be as good here

runners.name: Justus
runners.spotlight: Out of form last season but seemed to have turned a corner when second off much-reduced mark at Hexham (2m, good) 16 days ago; player if in similar mood again here.
runners.comment: Seemed to have turned a corner when second off much-reduced mark at Hexham last month

runners.name: Genbu
runners.spotlight: Came good for Sarah-Jayne Davies at third attempt, holding on well to make all over C&D (good) last month; 4lb higher now but won't mind if there's rain about; respected in current form.
runners.comment: Came good for Sarah-Jayne Davies at third attempt, making all over C&D in May; respected

runners.name: Clever Relation
runners.spotlight: Won on the Flat for Hughie Morrison in 2024 but was soundly beaten at triple-digit odds in three novice hurdles for Sheila Lewis in 2024-25 season; makes stable/handicap hurdle debut after 462-day absence; could be considered only if lively in the betting.
runners.comment: Makes stable/handicap debut after long absence; considered only if lively in betting

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden (IRE Incentive Race)

runners.name: Margot Mae
runners.spotlight: Starspangledbanner filly a 115,000gns yearling; half-sister to winners incl' New Endeavour (7f-1m1f inc 2yo/AW/Australian Group 3; RPR 117), and Johnny Boom (8.6f-1m4f inc AW; 80); dam unraced half-sister to 7f 2yo Group 3 winner Luminous Eyes; plenty keen and green on Navan debut in late April; given a break since and more expected of.
runners.comment: Plenty keen and green on Navan debut in late April; given a break since, more expected of

runners.name: Ibelieveicanfly
runners.spotlight: Frankel filly is closely related to 1m-10.6f winner Piz Badile (inc 2yo/AW/Group 3; RPR 116), half-sister to 6f/7f winner Yosemite Valley (inc Listed; 117); dam 1m1f-10.5f winner (inc AW/Listed; 105), out of 10.5f Group 1 winner from fine family; Ryan Moore's pick and yard have won this for the last three years.
runners.comment: Frankel filly closely related to smart 1m-10.6f winner Piz Badile
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier & Flaxman S

runners.name: Carafinn
runners.spotlight: Mehmas filly cost 120,000euros as a yearling; half-sister to winners Warriors Whisper (French Group-placed 9.5f AW; RPR 98) and Carencey (6f/7f; 82); dam 7f-1m2f winner (inc Group 3; 105), sister to dam of top-class middle-distance performer Pyledriver; watch for market support on debut.
runners.comment: Mehmas filly cost 120,000euros as a yearling; watch for market support on debut

runners.name: Alpha
runners.spotlight: Sea The Stars filly is closely related to 1m2f-1m6f winner Saturn (inc AW; RPR 98), half-sister to 7.5f winner Proxima Centauri (87); dam 6f-1m winner (inc 2yo/Irish 1,000 Guineas; 124), half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Alpine Star, from fine family of Miesque; super pedigree.
runners.comment: Sea The Stars filly from top family, Moore prefers Ibelieveicanfly.
runners.owner: Flaxman Stables, Mrs Magnier, M Tabor, D Smith

runners.name: Blonde Over Blue
runners.spotlight: Lope De Vega filly cost 260,000gns as a foal and 400,000gns as a yearling; first foal; dam 1m1f-1m4f winner (inc AW/Italian Group 3; RPR 105), half-sister to Japanese 1m2f winner Steen; newcomer represents another top yard, has to be noted for market support.
runners.comment: 400,000gns Lope De Vega yearling; top yard, has to be noted for market support on debut

runners.name: Honey Deuce
runners.spotlight: St Mark's Basilica filly cost 90,000euros as a yearling; half-sister to winners Magda (French 8.5f 2yo/1m2f) and Dubai First (Norwegian 1m4f); dam French 10.5f-12.5f winner (inc AW/Listed; RPR 105), out of unraced half-sister to Breeders' Cup Juvenile winner Wilko; watch market.
runners.comment: 90,000euros St Mark's Basilica filly; watch market

runners.name: Littlepacer
runners.spotlight: Dark Angel filly cost 23,000euros as a foal and 20,000euros as a yearling; half-sister to winners Havana Rum (5f/6f inc 2yo/AW; RPR 87) and Armada Rising (US 1m turf); dam unraced half-sister to US 1m Grade 1 winner Hunt.
runners.comment: Dark Angel half-sister to 2yo 5f/6f winner Havana Rum

runners.name: Pink Venom
runners.spotlight: Soldier's Call filly a 85,000euros yearling; third foal; half-sister to 6f/7f 2yo winner Rebel Diamond (inc Listed/Qatar; RPR 98); dam unraced half-sister to winners Sollertia (7f 2yo), The Ginger Queen (US 7.5f/1m) and Szachrajka (Polish 7f/1m); debutante likely all the better for the experience.
runners.comment: Soldier's Call filly an 85,000euros yearling;  likely all the better for the experience

runners.name: Westminster Waltz
runners.spotlight: Churchill filly cost 25,000euros as a yearling; half-sister to four winners inc Take Me With You (Group-placed 6f 2yo; RPR 96), Just Ain't Right (US stakes-placed 6f dirt) and Is It True Ma'am (US 6f dirt 2yo); unraced dam out of US 6f Grade 3 2yo winner; debutante probably best watched.
runners.comment: Half-sister to Group-placed 2yo 6f winner Take Me With You; debutante likely best watched

runners.name: Alpine Wedding
runners.spotlight: Sioux Nation filly cost 115,000euros as a foal and 220,000euros as a yearling; sister to 6.5f and 7f (French Group 3) 2yo winner Sydneyarms Chelsea (RPR 96), half-sister to 6f AW winner Bucklow Hill (74); dam 6f AW 2yo winner (75), out of Group-placed 6f 2yo winner; top yard, obvious appeal on debut.
runners.comment: 220,000euros Sioux Nation filly; top yard, obvious appeal on debut

runners.name: Chiquitita Bee
runners.spotlight: State Of Rest filly; 12,000euros yearling; second foal; dam runner-up 6f 2yo (RPR 82), half-sister to Italian 1m7f jumps winner Teddy's Profit, out of French 1m2f winning half-sister to 5f/5.5f 2yo Group 2 winner Signora Cabello; plenty keen, dropped out to finish last of 9 on recent Cork debut; this a stronger maiden, easily passed over.
runners.comment: Well held on recent Cork debut; this a stronger maiden, easily passed over

runners.name: Mallavelly
runners.spotlight: Progressive in handicaps last year; went up an aggregate 11lb for wins here and Tipperary and ended season with a fine third in a premier handicap at this venue (Bella Isabella, seventh); sights lowered again after a 1m Group 3 bid here on return; definite chance.
runners.comment: Progressive in handicaps last year, sights lowered again after a Group 3 bid on return

runners.name: Dark Viper
runners.spotlight: Has recorded his only two wins over 1m at this venue; ground was heavy when he scored off this mark last October, his 2024 win was on good going; his record of finishing second ten times strikes a note of caution; below best in three 2026 appearances.
runners.comment: Has recorded his only two wins over 1m at this venue, second ten times, below best lately

runners.name: Send Harry
runners.spotlight: Dependable on turf last summer, when his form included two Curragh placings over 6f; subsequently won over today's trip on Dundalk AW; possible that he will step up on the form of last month's outings at the Curragh and Cork.
runners.comment: Dependable on turf last summer, Dundalk winner, fair chance on last year's form

runners.name: Thru And Thru
runners.spotlight: Lightly-raced filly; second twice in maidens and made a bright start in handicaps when third of 12 over 7f at Naas in March; something seemingly amiss when favourite here in April, though vet's report revealed nothing; may bounce back now.
runners.comment: Maiden with fair placed form, something seemingly amiss when favourite here in April

runners.name: Mehman
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW winner, sole turf success came in 5f handicap at Naas in May 2024; solid effort when over 1m at Bellewstown in April and backed that up with a close third over 6f at Naas last month; 1-22 record on turf is a concern but those recent efforts have been encouraging
runners.comment: Four AW wins, 1-22 record on turf is a concern but recent efforts have been encouraging

runners.name: Playin Cool
runners.spotlight: Ended last season with a 1m1f course maiden win on heavy ground; yet to make an impact in handicaps; trip might be inadequate.
runners.comment: Course maiden winner over 1m1f last October, yet to make an impact in handicaps

runners.name: Great Mover
runners.spotlight: Only win from 20 starts came on heavy ground at Gowran last September; has failed to retrieve her best form in four outings this term, though she was aimed high in the Irish Lincoln and was not beaten far in sixth over 1m at Limerick on her penultimate start; down in trip again after a 1m2f run here last time, eased when beaten.
runners.comment: Only one win from 20 starts, has failed to retrieve her best form in four races this term

runners.name: Loingseoir
runners.spotlight: Durable veteran, 7-74, placed 13 times; recorded his most recent win last autumn; best form at the Curragh and Fairyhouse; makes a rare visit to Leopardstown, placed on both starts here; two mid-division runs this season; retains ability
runners.comment: Durable veteran, 7-74, placed 13 times, two mid-division runs this season, retains ability
runners.owner: W Keeling & M C Grassick & Mrs M J Grassick

runners.name: Bella Isabella
runners.spotlight: Cork maiden winner (7f, good) last season went on to take third of eight in a  Group 3 next time (7f, good); finished around 2l behind today's rival Mallavelly (third) when seventh of 20 in a 1m premier handicap here on her final start last term; well beaten on seasonal debut at Naas
runners.comment: Career-best when third in a C&D Group 3 event last season, well beaten on seasonal debut

runners.name: Flaming Rib
runners.spotlight: Seven wins in Britain; runner-up for Hugo Palmer in 2022 Commonwealth Cup at Royal Ascot; regressive since 2023; improved from a moderate stable debut last September when close fourth in a 7f rated at Dundalk in November; last of ten at the same venue in December; rated 113 at his peak, now down to 79 but remains risky.
runners.comment: Seven wins and second in 2022 Commonwealth Cup, regressive and remains risky

runners.name: Eddie G
runners.spotlight: Two AW wins, 0-11 on turf; ran only twice last season; denied a clear run when third of 14 on his seasonal reappearance in first-time tongue time at Roscommon; failed to build on that Cork eight days later and absent since.
runners.comment: Two AW wins, 0-11 on turf, ran only twice last season, good effort on the first occasion

runners.name: Endorsement
runners.spotlight: Second to the current Derby favourite Benvenuto Cellini on debut at Killarney; best display at two when second to Pierre Bonnard in the Zetland Stakes; this year's course form involves links to Derby candidates; stuck to his task to take second when Pierre Bonnard flopped in the Ballysax; pick of these on his close third behind James J Braddock and Pierre Bonnard in the Derby Trial.
runners.comment: Boasts form linked to several key Derby contenders, can make the most of this opportunity

runners.name: Amadeus Mozart
runners.spotlight: Made a winning debut in a 7f maiden at the Curragh last June; no match for Godolphin's Maho Bay in a novice event at Newmarket in April (winner a beaten favourite in the Lingfield Derby Trial on next start); looked in need of further when failing to make an impact in the 1m2f Gallinule Stakes won by stablemate Causeway.
runners.comment: Trip should be within his range but stablemate Endorsement has a stronger profile
runners.owner: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier

runners.name: Yousaynothingatall
runners.spotlight: Handicap mark unchanged on 80 after a maiden win at Cork a month on her fifth start; safely held by Emmeleia on running at Dundalk in April.
runners.comment: 80-rated maiden winner, same handicap mark as So Must I but likely more progressive

runners.name: So Must I
runners.spotlight: Winner of a middle-distance Dundalk conditions race in February; subsequent form in turf handicaps does not inspire confidence at this level.
runners.comment: AW winner,  form in turf handicaps does not inspire confidence at this level.

races.race_name: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Median Auction Fillies Maiden

runners.name: Fleur De Provence
runners.spotlight: Easy to back 16-1, made a promising debut over 1m at Naas in late March when staying on to finish second behind the Aidan O'Brien-trained favourite Drop Dead Gorgeous, despite running green; failed to uphold form with fourth-placed Sanctijude at Gowran last month; longer trip may make the difference now.
runners.comment: Runner-up in 1m maidens at Naas and Gowran, this longer trip may make the difference now

runners.name: Freedom And Light
runners.spotlight: Ordinary form in maidens; ran poorly in a conditions race at Naas last time. eased after chance had gone; officially rated 9lb inferior to Fleur De Provence.
runners.comment: Ran poorly at Naas last time, Officially rated 9lb inferior to Fleur De Provence

runners.name: Bang Bang
runners.spotlight: Slow to break but shaped reasonably well over 7f here on debut last July when staying on strongly into fifth, having run green; not beaten far when fifth of nine over the same trip at the Galway festival; no match for the well-fancied Mixed Feelings in a 1m maiden here in April; not guaranteed to uphold form with fourth-placed Lake Bonney.
runners.comment: Second here in April, not guaranteed to uphold form with fourth-placed Lake Bonney

runners.name: Lake Bonney
runners.spotlight: Encouraging effort on debut over 1m at this venue in April, keeping on from midfield for fourth of 14; will be no surprise if she improved sufficiently to turn the tables on the runner-up Bang Bang.
runners.comment: Encouraging debut at this venue, may improve enough to turn the tables on Bang Bang
runners.owner: Barry Fowler & Mrs A M O'Brien

runners.name: Mawhibah
runners.spotlight: Tenth foal; Australia filly is closely related to a minor handicap winner, much more interesting as a half-sister to Group 1 sprint winner Librisa Breeze; dam 1m2f winner.
runners.comment: Interesting as a half-sister to Group 1 sprint winner Librisa Breeze, dam 1m2f winner.
runners.owner: Mohammed Bin Hamad Khalifa Al Attiyah

runners.name: My Girl Grace
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; half-sister by Ghaiyyath to multiple winners Croupier (four races, RPR 108) and Touchwood (five races, RPR 83); dam unraced sister to 7f 2yo Group 2 winner Saamidd.
runners.comment: Half-sister to two multiple winners, dam unraced sister to 7f 2yo Group 2 winner Saamidd

runners.name: Shanala
runners.spotlight: Looked very raw when introduced at Gowran last month; likely to improve but perhaps not by enough to win here.
runners.comment: Likely to improve from an initial run at Gowran but perhaps not by enough to win here
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Ventriloquist
runners.spotlight: E8,500 yearling; fourth foal; half-sister to winners, notably Serious Challenge (Listed-placed and also a useful hurdler), and Group 3 sprint winner Dawn Charger.
runners.comment: Half-sister to the versatile Serious Challenge and Group 3 sprint winner Dawn Charger

runners.name: An Cailleach
runners.spotlight: Completely tailed off first time out at Gowran eight weeks ago; can be ignored.
runners.comment: Completely tailed off first time out at Gowran eight weeks ago, can be ignored

runners.name: Churchfield Lady
runners.spotlight: Pedigree implies she might have a stamina issue over this trip; that view is endorsed by her debut run at Cork when she ran out of steam over a furlong down.
runners.comment: Pedigree implies she might have a stamina issue over this trip, ran out of steam at Cork

runners.name: Path To Peace
runners.spotlight: Slowly away, never in contention when a 66-1 chance on debut here last month; visored now.
runners.comment: Slowly away, never in contention when a 66-1 chance on debut here last month, visored now

runners.name: Tintinabulla
runners.spotlight: 100-1, never involved first time out at the Curragh; highly unlikely to feature.
runners.comment: 100-1, never involved first time out at the Curragh, highly unlikely to feature

runners.name: Stellar Moment
runners.spotlight: Failed to cope with testing ground first time out at the Curragh in March; best watched.
runners.comment: Failed to cope with testing ground first time out at the Curragh in March, best watched

runners.name: Donna Time
runners.spotlight: Left an impression she would improve from the run when introduced at Dundalk in January; beaten around 7l in seventh in what looked like an ordinary contest
runners.comment: Beaten around 7l in seventh in what looked like an ordinary contest at Dundalk in January

races.race_name: BOYLE Sports 'Home Of The Early Payout' Handicap

runners.name: Johnny Soda
runners.spotlight: Breakthrough win at Cork (1m, soft) last month when coming late (beating a subsequent winner); didn't appear to stay 1m2f at Navan 13 days later, this intermediate trip could suit.
runners.comment: Didn't appear to stay 1m2f at Navan, this intermediate trip could suit

runners.name: Sunrush
runners.spotlight: Last month's seasonal return here (1m) was okay but nothing to suggest he'll make an immediate impact in handicaps off this mark, albeit has to be respected from a yard having a great season.
runners.comment: Yard in fine form but may need more time to make an impact in handicaps

runners.name: Son Of Beauty
runners.spotlight: AW maiden winner over 1m in early March and just fair efforts since in Naas handicaps, beaten around 5l in Madrid Handicap (7f) and stayed on late over 1m when beaten under 4l in premier handicap in late April; up another furlong in trip here and needs to improve for it.
runners.comment: Needs to improve on opening two handicap runs, longer trip may help

runners.name: Red Autumn
runners.spotlight: Won Bellewstown maiden last year and decent second in Roscommon winners' contest, best efforts; encouraging comeback run in Madrid Handicap at Naas in late March, staying on late into fourth over inadequate 7f; step up in trip seems sure to suit and top-weight should go well.
runners.comment: Naas comeback run over inadequate trip encouraging; longer trip to suit, should go well

runners.name: Monvoe
runners.spotlight: Rapid improver of late, twice runner-up on AW last autumn and has reappeared for back-to-back wins in 1m handicaps at Limerick and when running away late on under today's rider here last month; every chance of staying this extra furlong and could still have more to offer despite 16lb rise for two wins.
runners.comment: Hat-trick seeker up 16lb but remains a big player with rider's claim a positive

runners.name: How'sthebai
runners.spotlight: Off the mark in 1m AW maiden in October on last of three juvenile outings; very promising seasonal return in 1m Naas premier handicap in late April, staying on well in closing stages when beaten 1l into fourth (Son Of Beauty 2.75l away fifth); big player given normal improvement and longer trip.
runners.comment: Very encouraging Naas seasonal return; major player if staying longer trip

runners.name: Ryefield Dasher
runners.spotlight: Cheaply bought Time Test colt showed fair form in three outings late last season, latter two in good-class Curragh maidens when intimating a step up in trip would suit; lack of a recent run a concern here on seasonal return and handicap debut.
runners.comment: Likely more to offer this year but lack of a recent run a concern on handicap debut

races.race_name: BOYLE Sports Best Odds From 8am Handicap

runners.name: Molto Amichi
runners.spotlight: Maiden placed several times last year including over C&D off 77; last month's comeback run here (1m) should have brought him on.
runners.comment: C&D second last year off 4lb higher; last month's comeback run should have brought him on

runners.name: Coul Dreamer
runners.spotlight: Winning hurdler but 0-9 on the Flat, placed in Clonmel maiden over today's trip last season and more recently staying on third over 1m in Killarney maiden last month; needs to improve on previous handicap efforts though.
runners.comment: Trip and ground to suit but maiden needs to improve on previous handicap efforts
runners.owner: Mrs Suzanne McAuley & Ms C A Jennings

runners.name: No More Porter
