runners.spotlight: All three Curragh wins have been over shorter; has run okay on occasions over this sort of trip but opposable on balance of form and draw not great.
runners.comment: All wins over shorter, remains opposable over this trip

runners.name: Cooley's Mist
runners.spotlight: Fourth of 21 on C&D handicap debut last summer was his best run for Natalia Lupini; no show on last month's Roscommon comeback, entitled to improve for that but an unlikely one.
runners.comment: Good C&D run last summer but plenty needed on recent comeback effort

runners.name: Pillar Of Hope
runners.spotlight: Five turf wins, most recently here over 1m2f last October off 70; yet to hit form this season however and remains opposable.
runners.comment: 1m2f winner here last year; yet to hit form this season however and remains opposable

runners.name: Diamond Exchange
runners.spotlight: Nicely progressive filly won AW maiden in January and has returned with good efforts in cheekpieces in 1m2f handicaps here, most recently asserting well inside final furlong for head win; 4lb rise okay, needs to find more.
runners.comment: Narrow 1m2f winner here last month; 4lb rise okay, needs to find more
runners.owner: Bronsan Racing & H Redknapp & John Breslin

runners.name: Booyea
runners.spotlight: Best run last year when second in valuable C&D handicap (good to firm) in first-time cheekpieces off 90; form regressed afterwards but encouraging seasonal return (well-backed) at Naas last month over 1m; down in grade here off a fair mark so one to consider.
runners.comment: C&D second last summer off 5lb higher; encouraging seasonal return and drops in grade here

runners.name: Lahore Da King
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back AW winner in December over 7f and landed Bellewstown handicap (1m, soft) in April but well held both starts since, not looking to stay extended 1m2f at Roscommon latest; opposable off current mark.
runners.comment: Wins have been over shorter; opposable off current mark

runners.name: Daler
runners.spotlight: Got first career win with comfortable 1m Dundalk handicap success in October off 72; just fair efforts on AW afterwards and turf return at Naas last month was just okay (keen); cheekpieces now added.
runners.comment: Turf return at Naas last month was just okay (keen); cheekpieces now added

runners.name: Harana
runners.spotlight: Very lightly raced 4yo; promising debut second in 1m Gowran maiden last year, absent for 13 months until third in Roscommon maiden last month over 1m4f, weakening late; back in trip for handicap debut with potential for improvement.
runners.comment: Unexposed handicap debutante with likely more to offer
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Walhaan
runners.spotlight: Seven Flat wins include over C&D, and more recently on the AW in December; trip inadequate on seasonal return here in April and has since been pulled up over hurdles; champion jockey booked but veteran appears vulnerable.
runners.comment: C&D winner but recent form not that encouraging; champion jockey booked

runners.name: Majestic King
runners.spotlight: Well-bred Godolphin cast-off progressing well in handicaps of late, ready winner over 1m at Gowran and three days later staying on second under hefty weight over today's trip at Ballinrobe; runs off 7lb higher mark here so needs to take another step forward, but possible.
runners.comment: Progressive 5yo of late, 7lb higher than when second at Ballinrobe so more needed

runners.name: Lough Leane
runners.spotlight: All six wins in Britain came on the AW; first success on turf came last month over this trip at Ballinrobe, quickening off a slow pace, despite racing keenly; 6lb rise but remains on a workable mark.
runners.comment: Recent Ballinrobe winner up 6lb but remains on a workable mark

runners.name: Elusive Duke
runners.spotlight: C&D winner a year ago for Kevin Coleman off 58; absent since beaten favourite at Naas in August so may need this.
runners.comment: C&D winner a year ago; absent since beaten favourite at Naas in August so may need this

runners.name: Sonoran
runners.spotlight: Won 7f Tipperary maiden on debut a year ago; hasn't won since in seven starts although best effort here last month when close third over 1m2f; 2lb higher and wide draw a negative.
runners.comment: Recent second here over 1m2f bodes well, although draw a negative

runners.name: Gotta Catch'em All
runners.spotlight: 24-race maiden has been running respectably this season but hard to see him breaking his duck here.
runners.comment: 24-race maiden running respectably this season but hard to see him breaking his duck
runners.owner: Mrs S Bury & Ralph Kelly & Odhran O'Neill & Thomas Cox

runners.name: Down The Glen
runners.spotlight: 7f Galway nursery winner on heavy ground last season; extended 1m2f seemed too far at Sligo on latest, so should appreciate this drop back in distance.
runners.comment: Didn't stay 1m2f at Sligo, drop in trip should suit

runners.name: Chirac
runners.spotlight: Has taken well to handicapping, good fourth to an improver over 5f at Naas before relishing step up to 7f for ready success at Gowran (soft) in April, coming late off a good pace; good chance he'll stay 1m and 7lb rise looks fair; decent draw too.
runners.comment: Much improved for step up to 7f at Gowran; good chance he'll stay 1m and 7lb rise fair

runners.name: Cosmic Funk
runners.spotlight: Last Friday's step up in trip at Down Royal didn't bring any improvement; back in trip for handicap debut (down 2lb in future).
runners.comment: Step up in trip at Down Royal didn't bring improvement; back in trip for handicap bow

runners.name: Wicklow Gap
runners.spotlight: Last month's Roscommon run his best yet, good fourth after not clear run in first-time tongue-tie; claims if overcoming this wide draw.
runners.comment: Roscommon fourth augurs well if able to overcome this wide draw

runners.name: Only One Scobie
runners.spotlight: Beaten only a couple of rivals in two handicap efforts; handicapper relenting but remains opposable.
runners.comment: Beaten only a couple of rivals in two handicap efforts; remains opposable

runners.name: De Songbird
runners.spotlight: No real threat in three maiden runs within a month in the spring; wide draw for handicap debut and hard to make a case for.
runners.comment: Wide draw for handicap debut and hard to make a case for

runners.name: Glamazon
runners.spotlight: Well exposed before Ballinrobe maiden win last month (1m1f) and faded tamely after forcing much of pace back in handicap company at the Curragh when racing from 7lb out of the handicap; drops in grade but remains vulnerable to improvers.
runners.comment: Stiff task last time; drops in grade but remains vulnerable to improvers

runners.name: Jp's Express
runners.spotlight: All runs have been over shorter trips, never counting after slow start on handicap debut at Naas last time; upped in trip and dropped 7lb; poor draw.
runners.comment: No show on handicap debut after slow start; down 7lb and upped in trip but poor draw

runners.name: Love Orchid
runners.spotlight: Minor promise in maidens last year but opening two handicap efforts this season have been ordinary; first-time cheekpieces will need to bring improvement to feature.
runners.comment: Opening handicap efforts ordinary; first-time cheekpieces will need to bring improvement

runners.name: Nicky Larson
runners.spotlight: Good start to handicapping when chasing home a clear-cut winner over this trip at Limerick in April (yielding); just 1lb higher and rider now claims 7lb so one to consider.
runners.comment: Limerick handicap debut second augurs well; rider's claim a positive too

runners.name: Cameclose
runners.spotlight: Back in trip here after not looking to stay at Killarney; cheekpieces replace blinkers; others preferred.
runners.comment: Back in trip here after not looking to stay at Killarney; cheekpieces replace blinkers;

runners.name: Almeiyda
runners.spotlight: Didn't appear to stay extended 1m2f at Gowran last month in what looked a decent maiden; could still have more to offer and capable of going well off this opening mark.
runners.comment: Back in trip for handicap debut off a fair mark; potential for more remains
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late H H Aga Khan

runners.name: Two And Two
runners.spotlight: Made all in 6f Dundalk 3yo contest in January and showed speed (hooded) for a long way in Curragh handicap over same trip on opening day of season before fading in heavy ground; back from a break and upped in trip with headgear left off.
runners.comment: AW 6f winner; back from a break and upped in trip with headgear left off

runners.name: Bear Right
runners.spotlight: Yet to make an impact in handicaps, not looking to get home on soft over this trip at Cork on latest; plenty to find.
runners.comment: Yet to make an impact in handicaps, plenty to find

runners.name: Themis
runners.spotlight: Three runs have amounted to little, poor run at Dundalk last time; Irish Oaks entry is unrealistic but evidently held in some regard so could make an impact on handicap debut off a modest mark.
runners.comment: Irish Oaks entry unrealistic but could make an impact on handicap debut off a modest mark

runners.name: No More Hiding
runners.spotlight: Tried over different trips in four AW runs over the winter, final occasion hung badly left in straight when well held on handicap debut; Billy Lee takes over for turf debut, interesting if any market support arrives.
runners.comment: Billy Lee takes over for turf debut, interesting if any market support arrives

runners.name: Adelaide Bay
runners.spotlight: Three 7f wins on Polytrack and Tapeta between April and June in 2025 for this yard; right out of luck for different trainers from November onwards but he fell a long way in the weights and shot home to take a late second at Southwell (7f) 14 days ago; should make hay off current mark, 6lb lower than latest win, if overcoming a wide draw.
runners.comment: Three 7f AW wins in 2025; 6lb lower than latest; close second back with former yard in May

runners.name: Yehudi
runners.spotlight: Has done most of his AW racing on Wolverhampton Tapeta but he's run his two best races on Kempton Polytrack (7f), going close in February and breaking his duck last month; back up 4lb; a likely contender on course debut.
runners.comment: Just two Polytrack runs, going close and winning over 7f at Kempton; likely contender

runners.name: Split Elevens
runners.spotlight: Three C&D wins but perhaps even more effective on Tapeta; had the benefit of Jack Callan's claim when successful at Wolverhampton (7f) last month and although back up 3lb he's perfectly capable of finding another race off this mark.
runners.comment: Three C&D wins; latest success at Wolverhampton under J Callan for first time; back up 3lb

runners.name: Fort Augustus
runners.spotlight: Won two of last three at Southwell (7f) since these aids were fitted, a steady late surge taking him to the front close home 14 days ago; acts on Polytrack, too, but plenty needs to go right today from a wide draw.
runners.comment: Won 2 of last 3 starts at Southwell since these aids fitted; acts on Polytrack; drawn wide

runners.name: Twitch
runners.spotlight: Often leads; tight between him and Francisco on C&D form in April but he couldn't hold his place on the turf track here since and this looks competitive, especially with others in the field who like to lead.
runners.comment: Often leads; tight with Francisco on C&D form in April but this looks more competitive
runners.owner: The Limes Of Epsom Racing Club

runners.name: Majestic Wave
runners.spotlight: Won 6f maiden at Navan as 2yo; promising yard debut over 6f on this track in February 2025 but he's mostly struggled since, thus the drop of 24lb in the weights since racing in Britain; should come on for his April reappearance and is one to keep an eye on for signs of a revival.
runners.comment: Disappointing run of form since good yard debut here; dropped a long way in the weights

runners.name: Francisco
runners.spotlight: Two 7f handicap wins on the AW, the latest over C&D in April under Charlie Tucker, but he was disappointing on the turf track here (7f) since, given that he's handled that test in the past; shade to prove this time.
runners.comment: Two 7f handicap wins on AW, the latest over C&D in April; disappointing on turf here since

runners.name: Oberon Hill
runners.spotlight: Signs of ability over 7f on Newcastle Tapeta but failed to stay 1m on handicap debut at Wetherby in April 2025; wasn't seen again until well beaten on Kempton AW (6f) in March for new yard, where he bled from the nose; best watched for now.
runners.comment: Signs of 7f ability on Tapeta; well beaten and bled from nose on yard debut in March

runners.name: Enpassant
runners.spotlight: Three 7f wins on AW elsewhere, most recently at Southwell in April; a quick return didn't pay off there next time and he was always behind when returned to turf at Leicester last month; has run well over C&D in the past, though, so he may be more himself this time.
runners.comment: Three 7f wins on AW elsewhere, the latest in April; lesser efforts in two starts since

runners.name: Dashing Donkey
runners.spotlight: All four wins from the front on turf, over 7f in 2024 and twice over 1m at Newmarket last summer; 0-9 on AW but he handles this surface and is not a forlorn hope on the return to 7f.
runners.comment: All four wins front-running on turf, at 7f and 1m; 0-9 on AW but acts on Polytrack

runners.name: Secret Road
runners.spotlight: Won at 6f and over C&D for James Owen at the start of the year, then over 1m here on yard debut in March, but his last three runs have been nothing like as good; has plenty to prove back at 7f.
runners.comment: Has won at 6f, 7f and 1m for different yards this year but well below best on last 3 runs

runners.name: Harbour Vision
runners.spotlight: Tremendous win record on AW, successful 16 times; however, the last 11 have been at Wolverhampton and he's gone off the boil since winning two in a row there (7f) at the start of the year; has it to prove in first Polytrack run for over a year.
runners.comment: Last 11 wins on Tapeta; has gone off the boil of late; first Polytrack run for a year

runners.name: Peregrine Falcon
runners.spotlight: Maiden after 14 runs, five under Billy Loughnane, but has been involved in tight finishes over 5f on Tapeta on three occasions so ought to find a race at some stage and perhaps this first go on Polytrack will suit.
runners.comment: 0-14 but involved in three tight finishes over 5f on Tapeta; first Polytrack run

runners.name: Desdemona
runners.spotlight: Tough filly who took time to find her form again but won two in a row in early spring, over C&D (overcame a wide draw) and at Chelmsford (5f), and subsequent defeats came over 6f then very slowly away here in April; form claims.
runners.comment: Two 5f wins in early spring, over C&D and at Chelmsford; had excuses since; form claims

runners.name: Shalaa Asker
runners.spotlight: Busy since the autumn; some narrow defeats to go with three 5f wins, on Polytrack elsewhere and Tapeta; ran flat at Southwell recently but he's realistically weighted if that wasn't a sign that he's going off the boil.
runners.comment: Been busy; many good runs at 5f over the winter include three 5f wins; below best latest

runners.name: Style King
runners.spotlight: Maiden; two poor efforts of late, with and without a hood, but he ran well for a long way behind a subsequent winner at Wolverhampton (6f) in April and is worth another go at 5f; first-time blinkers today.
runners.comment: Maiden; two poor efforts of late but worth another go at 5f; now blinkered

runners.name: Honour Your Dreams
runners.spotlight: Six turf wins, three in 2025; 0-22 on AW and although having a healthy pull at the weights with Desdemona on their C&D run in February he's not looked in peak form on last two starts.
runners.comment: Six turf wins; weights pull with Desdemona on C&D form in February but now 0-22 on AW

runners.name: Diamond Dreamer
runners.spotlight: C&D winner who came close to ending a losing AW run when a fast-closing runner-up here (5f) in March (5f); returns to this C&D for the first time since and ought to go well if the race falls his way.
runners.comment: C&D winner; came close to ending a losing AW run here (5f) in March; first C&D run since

runners.name: Balon d'Or
runners.spotlight: Both handicap wins on turf at Chester (5f and 6f); no great shakes on this track for new yard since November but he's dropped 9lb in that time and there was the glimmer of a revival here last time when back from a break; not ruled out.
runners.comment: Mostly well held here for new yard but down 9lb since November; glimmer of revival latest

races.race_name: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 'Confined' EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (2 Runs Or Less) (GBB/GBBPlus Race)

runners.name: Sunshine Star
runners.spotlight: Half-sister to connections' Oaks winner Soul Sister; close second to useful rival at Kempton (1m, AW) on sole 2yo start; managed only sixth when odds-on favourite for good Sandown novice (1m2f, good) in April but this race looks much easier and the return to Polytrack will suit; good chance.
runners.comment: Well-bred filly; only sixth when odds-on at Sandown but this race looks much easier

runners.name: Lightning Glory
runners.spotlight: Fair form when making the frame on both starts this spring, more recently when third of 11 at Southwell (1m); holds each-way claims at least if suited by the longer trip here.
runners.comment: Fair form when making the frame twice over 1m; holds each-way claims at least here
runners.owner: Duaij Al Khalifa & Ahmad Al Qassimi

runners.name: Kashooda
runners.spotlight: Fifth foal; sister to French 7f 2yo/9.7f winner Khadidja, half-sister to three winners including Kawida (7f/1m 2yo including Listed; RPR 106) and Khinjani (1m2f; 94); dam 9.4f winner (68), half-sister to Australian 1m1f/1m2f Group 1 winner Zaaki; in good hands; market may guide on debut.
runners.comment: Well-bred filly from a good stable; market helpful on debut

runners.name: Musical Accord
runners.spotlight: Midfield in fairly good Newbury maiden (1m2f, good) on debut in April but unable to land any sort of blow at Wolverhampton (9.4f) last month; needs to up her game here.
runners.comment: Midfield in good Newbury maiden in April but last month's AW run was a backward step

runners.name: Corallience
runners.spotlight: Not disgraced on 2yo debut at Newbury (1m, soft) but suffered heavy defeat when reappearing at Sandown (1m2f, good; behind Sunshine Star) six weeks ago.
runners.comment: Not disgraced on 2yo debut but suffered heavy defeat on reappearance

runners.name: Kenkelly
runners.spotlight: Probably didn't achieve a great deal when well-held third of five in race won easily by short-odds favourite at Yarmouth (11.5f, good to firm) in April and was soundly beaten when dropped to 1m2f at Goodwood last month.
runners.comment: Didn't achieve a great deal when third of five on debut; soundly beaten since

runners.name: Shopaholic
runners.spotlight: Made fairly encouraging debut when fifth of eight over C&D in April and may not have been suited by good to soft turf when below that form last month but others in this line-up look stronger.
runners.comment: Made fairly encouraging debut over C&D; ground a possible excuse last time

runners.name: Antiquarian
runners.spotlight: Struggled on 2yo debut (6f) and was almost certainly flattered by proximity to principals when sixth of seven in slowly run contest at Ascot (1m2f, good) last month.
runners.comment: Flattered by proximity when sixth in slowly run Ascot contest last month

runners.name: Northern Bea
runners.spotlight: 62,000gns yearling; eighth foal; closely related to 1m4f winner Royal Associate (RPR 99), half-sister to three winners including Kingfisher King (1m2f; 106) and Officer Of State (1m7f; 103); dam unplaced 1m-1m2f in France; makes debut in a race lacking strength in depth.
runners.comment: Closely related to a useful 1m4f winner; makes debut in race lacking strength in debut

runners.name: Hunting Berries
runners.spotlight: Well beaten at triple-digit odds on first two starts (6f/1m); upped in trip again here.
runners.comment: Well beaten at triple-digit odds on first two starts (6f/1m); upped in trip again here

runners.name: Blue Tulip
runners.spotlight: Has a good pedigree but was always behind when 100-1 for recent debut at Southwell (1m).
runners.comment: Always behind when 100-1 for recent debut at Southwell (1m)

runners.name: Arrivera
runners.spotlight: First foal from a useful 7f AW winner; showed only a glimmer of promise before weakening into sixth of seven on debut at Southwell (1m) on New Year's Day.
runners.comment: Showed only a glimmer of promise on debut at Southwell (1m) on New Year's Day

runners.name: Coral Cathedral
runners.spotlight: Out of a German Group 3 winner; too green to do herself justice when 33-1 for debut at Wolverhampton (8.6f) in December; needs to have made good progress in the meantime.
runners.comment: Too green to do herself justice when 33-1 for debut at Wolverhampton (8.6f) in December

runners.name: Extraterrestrial
runners.spotlight: Well down the field after racing too freely on turf last month but was in good form beforehand, most notably when close second at Southwell (1m3f; first-time cheekpieces) two runs ago; in the mix if back on song.
runners.comment: Below form last time (raced too freely) but in the mix if quickly back on song

runners.name: Raspoutine
runners.spotlight: Won in good style here (1m) on seasonal/handicap debut in April and made the frame on both starts since, more recently when upped to 1m2f at Salisbury 12 days ago; returns to this track with first-time blinkers fitted; may still have more improvement to come.
runners.comment: Course winner on seasonal/handicap debut and another two good runs since; blinkers on

runners.name: Booziebrunch
runners.spotlight: Three good runs over 1m this year, the latest in refitted blinkers at Kempton four weeks ago, but probably needs this longer trip to unlock some improvement.
runners.comment: Three good runs over 1m this season; needs to find a bit more over this new trip

runners.name: Lexington Express
runners.spotlight: Front-runner with 3-6 strike-rate for new stable this year, the latest win gained in four-runner contest at Wolverhampton (9.4f) last month; enjoyed the run of the race that day and has gone up another 6lb but is evidently in excellent nick.
runners.comment: Dominated small field from the front last month and is now 3-6 for new stable this year

runners.name: Central Command
runners.spotlight: Needs to bounce back quickly from very disappointing turf debut at Haydock a fortnight ago but came from off the pace for emphatic win at Newcastle (1m2f) on seasonal/handicap debut in early May, and has obvious claims if judged on that performance.
runners.comment: Flopped badly on turf last time but was emphatic winner on seasonal/handicap debut

runners.name: Kaori
runners.spotlight: Made the frame on all three 2yo starts, most notably when third of 12 in Leicester maiden (7f, good to soft) in September; makes handicap/AW debut after 255-day absence but has plenty of scope for improvement in middle-distance handicaps this year.
runners.comment: Placed in 7f maiden in September; open to improvement in middle-distance handicaps
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late Sheikh Mohammed Obaid

races.race_name: Free Tips On attheraces.com Fillies' Handicap

runners.name: Polka Blue
runners.spotlight: Following a series of near misses, she got her head in front when it mattered, down in trip and back on turf at Leicester (6f, good to firm) nine days ago; equally effective at 7f; strong claims under 6lb penalty in follow-up bid.
runners.comment: Following a series of near misses, she got her head in front at Leicester nine days ago.

runners.name: Bella Bisbee
runners.spotlight: Landed her fourth AW win when scoring over C&D in April; has been beaten three times since at this track, twice on AW, and although she cannot be discounted she is more exposed than the rest of the field.
runners.comment: Landed fourth AW win when scoring over C&D in April; has been beaten three times since.

runners.name: Hamaleel
runners.spotlight: Made all to get off the mark at Kempton (1m, AW) in January; has run with credit employing same tactics three times since without managing to sustain her effort; first-time blinkers replace cheekpieces and likely to be the one to catch.
runners.comment: Made all to get off the mark at Kempton in January; has run with credit since.

runners.name: Doodling
runners.spotlight: 0-4 but was only beaten a neck on her second start/AW debut at Kempton (1m) in April; two lesser runs since, including on handicap debut when eighth of 14 at Salisbury (7f, good) 12 days ago; first-time cheekpieces need to have the desired effect.
runners.comment: 0-4 but was only beaten a neck at Kempton in April; two lesser runs since.

runners.name: Pretty Danielle
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden but wasn't beaten far when third over C&D in March; may have had an off-day when beaten 18l at Leicester (7f, good to firm) the following month; sports first-time blinkers and impossible to write off.
runners.comment: Eight-race maiden but wasn't beaten far when third over C&D in March.

runners.name: Startled Lady
runners.spotlight: Has made the frame six times but remains a maiden; needs to show a shade more in order to bag her first win.
runners.comment: Has made the frame six times but remains a maiden; needs to show a shade more.

runners.name: Ritaal
runners.spotlight: Runner-up, beaten around 5l, in two Wolverhampton fillies' maidens (7f/6f) in November when trained by Owen Burrows; sold for 28,000gns the following month but two lesser runs for new yard since; wears first-time cheekpieces and has something to prove now.
runners.comment: Runner-up in a couple of Wolverhampton maidens last backend, but two lesser runs since.

runners.name: Diamondinthedark
runners.spotlight: Off the mark on her second start, at Wolverhampton (6f, AW) in November; steps up in trip on handicap debut on first run since and may well show improvement; market will be best guide.
runners.comment: Off the mark on her second start, at Wolverhampton in November; handicap debut.

races.race_name: Download The At The Races App EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Band D) (Qual') (GBB) (Div I)

runners.name: Fire Thunder
runners.spotlight: Showed plenty when runner-up in similar events at Wolverhampton and Windsor last month; looks like a winner waiting to happen and must be on the shortlist.
runners.comment: Showed plenty when runner-up in similar events at Wolverhampton and Windsor last month.

runners.name: Tricky Jenny
runners.spotlight: Beaten 12l when fifth of ten on her debut at Musselburgh (5f, soft) in April but showed more when sixth of eight at Ripon (6f, good) last month; holds place claims.
runners.comment: Showed more when sixth at Ripon last month; holds place claims.

runners.name: Crystal Kraken
runners.spotlight: Showing steady improvement and was third of 15 at Redcar (6f, good; 100-1) last month on her latest start; needs more in order to win one of these but will be of interest when the nursery season starts.
runners.comment: Showing steady improvement, third at Redcar last month on latest start.

runners.name: Defiant Dream
runners.spotlight: 1,000gns yearling; second foal; by Dream Ahead; dam 7f winner (RPR 70), half-sister to winners Kiniro (6f), Perfect Charm (5f), Inflamed (1m/8.6f 2yo) and Lucio Vero (French 1m6f/15.5f), out of 6f Listed winner; yard's first 2yo runner of the season and likely to need time.
runners.comment: Yard's first 2yo runner of the season and likely to need time.

runners.name: Doha Rd
runners.spotlight: Slowly away in two runs at Bath (5f, good); steps up in trip on AW debut and needs to break on terms.
runners.comment: Slowly away in two runs at Bath; steps up in trip and needs to break on terms.

runners.name: River In Paradise
runners.spotlight: 10,000gns yearling; first foal; by Lope Y Fernandez; dam 1m AW winner (RPR 98), half-sister to useful 1m2f-1m4f winner Look Closely, out of 1m 2yo Listed winning half-sister to US 1m/8.5f Grade 3 winner Thewayiam; makes debut and likely to need more time.
runners.comment: 10,000gns yearling; makes debut and likely to need more time.

runners.name: Rocsane
runners.spotlight: Beaten 13l on debut at Yarmouth (6f, good to soft) last month; makes AW debut and will know more this time.
runners.comment: Beaten 13l on debut at Yarmouth last month; makes AW debut.

runners.name: It'sbeenemotional
runners.spotlight: Showed ability when fourth of ten on her debut at Kempton (6f, AW; 9-1) 15 days ago, gradually getting the hang of things as the race developed; should be sharper with that run under her belt.
runners.comment: Showed ability when fourth on her debut at Kempton 15 days ago.

runners.name: Holliesthedollie
runners.spotlight: Hung left throughout when beaten 12l on her debut at Bath (5.5f, firm; 20-1) in April; steps up in trip on AW debut and needs to show improvement.
runners.comment: Hung left throughout when beaten 12l on her debut at Bath in April.

runners.name: Sparkling Jem
runners.spotlight: Always behind when beating only one rival at Windsor (6f, good to firm; 80-1) 12 days ago; AW debut; others preferred.
runners.comment: Always behind when beating only one rival at Windsor 12 days ago.

races.race_name: Download The At The Races App EBF Restricted Maiden Fillies' Stakes (Band D) (Qual') (GBB) (Div II)

runners.name: Ron's Angel
runners.spotlight: Friendless in the market for Bath debut (5f) in May but she travelled strongly and found only one with previous experience too good (the pair clear of the third who has run well since); should cope with this sharp 6f now switched to AW; good claims under Rossa Ryan who's 9-25 for yard in recent years.
runners.comment: 33-1 for Bath debut but travelled strongly and did well in 2nd; should stay 6f on AW debut

runners.name: Forest Berry
runners.spotlight: Most of her pedigree suggests 1m+ should suit but her dam won at about 6f in the US and this 2yo showed plenty of dash when runner-up at Chepstow (6f) recently on her second start; should improve further on this return to Polytrack; can be involved again.
runners.comment: Showed plenty of dash when runner-up at Chepstow (6f) on second start; can improve further

runners.name: Potters Margot
runners.spotlight: March foal; seventh foal; Lope Y Fernandez half-sister to winners Gigi's Beach (6f-1m AW; RPR 70) and Ciyounesse (German 1m/8.5f including AW); dam US 1m turf 2yo winner (including stakes; 100); nice enough pedigree but likely to need a run or two.
runners.comment: Dam bred two AW winners; seems likely to be better for the run

runners.name: Clear Horizon
runners.spotlight: April foal; 12,000gns yearling; fourth foal; Without Parole sister to 1m4f winner Emily Rebecca (RPR 67), half-sister to 1m winner Dashing Darcey (including 2yo/AW; 98); dam 7f AW winner (58) from  family of fine sprinters Deacon Blues and The Tin Man; yard's newcomers can go well at a price on debut.
runners.comment: From family of fine sprinters; newcomers from yard can go well at a price on debut

runners.name: Zigazig Ah
runners.spotlight: February foal; 13,000gns yearling; fourth foal; Rajasinghe sister to winners Giant (7f/1m AW including 2yo; RPR 90) and I Follow Rivers (7f AW; 70); dam Listed-placed 6f/7f winner (including 2yo; 95); yard 2-26 with 2yos on the AW here; market can guide.
runners.comment: 13,000gns yearling; yard 2-26 with 2yos on AW here; market can guide

runners.name: Deja Vu Diva
runners.spotlight: Bred to be speedy but she achieved little on her Yarmouth debut (6f; 250-1) in May; one to watch for now.
runners.comment: Bred to be speedy but she achieved little on her Yarmouth debut (6f; 250-1) in May

runners.name: Dun Eideann
runners.spotlight: 25-1 for Wolverhampton AW debut (6f) 24 days ago and although never remotely involved after starting slowly, she did make some late inroads into sixth of 11; should know more this time and can improve.
runners.comment: 25-1 for Wolverhampton AW debut when slowly away but made late inroads into 6th

runners.name: Last Dandelion
runners.spotlight: 66-1 and remote on Kempton AW debut but there was much more evidence of ability when fifth of 13 at Newmarket (6f; 200-1) to a useful winner, looking likely to finish third until two rivals came past very late on; can improve further but handicaps may be her best option.
runners.comment: Promising 5th of 13 to useful winner at Newmarket (6f) on second start; has potential

runners.name: Sea Of Sardinia
runners.spotlight: Modest form when failing to beat a rival in 5f events at Newmarket (made the running and faded) and Yarmouth (first time tongue tied); minor handicaps on the agenda after today.
runners.comment: Big prices and failed to beat a rival in two 5f events; minor handicaps will suit better

runners.name: Happy Humpo
runners.spotlight: Minor form over 5f at Beverley and when dropped into a seller at Chester (6f; 20-1); has a lot to find in a maiden.
runners.comment: Modest form in two starts, dropped into a seller at Chester (6f; 20-1) last time

runners.name: Sail On Sailor
runners.spotlight: Placed on his first two starts for this yard before opening his account at Bath (1m, good) last month, leading in the final strides; more to do off 3lb higher on return to AW but may have more to give.
runners.comment: Scored at Bath last month after being placed on his first two starts for this yard.

runners.name: Fallacious Promise
runners.spotlight: Found one too good for the second time in succession up in trip at Newcastle (1m2f, AW; 5-4) last month; knocking on the door and this drop back to 1m could pay dividends.
runners.comment: Found one too good for second time in succession when up in trip at Newcastle last month.

runners.name: Lordsbridge Bay
runners.spotlight: Made steady progress in novice/maiden company but hasn't gone on in a couple of handicaps since, on latest start beaten 22l on turf debut here last month; first-time hood needs to being about improvement.
runners.comment: Made steady progress before failing to go on in a couple of handicaps; first-time hood.

runners.name: Case Study
runners.spotlight: Unplaced in four starts, including on handicap debut at Newmarket (1m, good; 28-1) last month; takes a drop in class in first-time cheekpieces and is in good hands, so there could be more to come from her.
runners.comment: Unplaced in four starts, including on handicap debut at Newmarket last month; cheekpieces.

runners.name: Angel Summer
runners.spotlight: Steady improvement on AW during the winter but reportedly lost her action when beaten 12l on turf/handicap debut at Yarmouth (6f, good to firm) in April; steps up in trip and needs to get back on track on this return to AW.
runners.comment: Reportedly lost her action when beaten 12l on turf/handicap debut at Yarmouth in April.

runners.name: Beryl's Girl
runners.spotlight: Consistent in her five runs to date; makes AW debut and she needs that bit extra in order to register her first win.
runners.comment: Consistent in her five runs to date; makes AW debut and she needs that bit extra.

runners.name: Moonlight Angel
runners.spotlight: Has beaten only three rivals in her three runs over 6f; steps up in trip on handicap debut and more is required from her.
runners.comment: Has beaten only three rivals in her three runs over 6f; up in trip on handicap debut.

runners.name: Ruiz
runners.spotlight: Has yet to finish ahead of a rival in three starts; steps up in trip in handicap debut but easy to dismiss.
runners.comment: Has yet to finish ahead of a rival in three starts; steps up in trip in handicap debut.

runners.name: Laurasia
runners.spotlight: Moderate debut and beaten 21l at 50-1 on her second start, after which she left James Owen for 1,000gns; last of eight last August on first start for this stable and big improvement is required up in trip on handicap debut.
runners.comment: Last of eight last August on first start for this stable and big improvement required.

runners.name: Mist Of Time
runners.spotlight: Down the field in three 7f AW runs last autumn; up in trip and needs to pull out more on this handicap debut.
runners.comment: Down the field in three 7f AW runs last autumn.

runners.name: Arishka's Dream
runners.spotlight: Bang in form on the AW before going off too fast when made favourite for his turf debut in a 6f handicap at Ripon; remains of interest and looks sharp enough for 5f; gelded since.
runners.comment: Running well on the AW until an excusable failure when favourite for turf debut

runners.name: Guernsey Lady
runners.spotlight: Good-ground novice winner last year; not her first placing in handicap company when a close third at Bath two weeks ago after 142 days off; possibilities.
runners.comment: Not her first placing in handicap company when a close third at Bath two weeks ago

runners.name: La Belle Forest
runners.spotlight: Ran in these colours for Karl Burke and was progressing when winning her AW maiden; her handicap debut back at Southwell was underwhelming given she went off favourite; probably better than that.
runners.comment: AW maiden winner who probably failed to give her running on handicap debut; new yard

runners.name: Beach Partee
runners.spotlight: Three wins for William Knight last autumn; might be on the way back after a fairly close fifth at Bath when just behind Guernsey Lady.
runners.comment: Three-time winner and might be on the way back after a fairly close fifth at Bath

runners.name: Arctic Wind
runners.spotlight: Promising runs for David Menuisier whom he left for 9,000gns; gelded ahead of stable debut at Wolverhampton where he was beaten 7l in a small field; may have needed that after a lengthy break and there are races in him.
runners.comment: Underwhelming yard debut but interesting on his promising form for previous stable

runners.name: Tie Fighter
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden who is making belated handicap debut; chance of opening his account largely depends on the effects of first-time headgear.
runners.comment: Seven-race maiden who is making belated handicap debut; new headgear

runners.name: Carefree Dream
runners.spotlight: Won small-field 5f handicap on soft ground last September; uncompetitive in two runs since on either side of a break.
runners.comment: Soft-ground winner; uncompetitive in last two races on either side of a break

runners.name: Alkuwarrior
runners.spotlight: Record of 1-11 overall; has finished last in two sprint handicaps for current yard; cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Has finished last in two sprint handicaps for current yard; cheekpieces added

runners.name: Real Trouble
runners.spotlight: Cost 105,000gns and already gelded; only weakened late on when beaten under 4l at Newcastle (6f) and improvement looks on the cards now raised to 7f; good chance according to RPRs.
runners.comment: Beaten under 4l at Newcastle and improvement on the cards now raised to 7f

runners.name: Persian Land
runners.spotlight: Outpaced for a long way at Haydock (6f, soft; 14-1) but the leaders stopped and he won going away; could have been flattered but clearly has a fair level of ability and Jack Dace's claim takes care of much of the penalty.
runners.comment: Got outpaced at Haydock (6f, soft) but the leaders stopped and he won going away

runners.name: Galipi
runners.spotlight: 72,000gns yearling; fifth foal; half-brother to four winners including Zillow (11.5f/1m4f Flat, RPR 92; useful 2m hurdle), Genetique (French 6.5f) and Energic (French 9.5f-1m4f); dam French 1m1f winner; bred to win races and the market can guide.
runners.comment: 72,000gns yearling; fifth foal; half-brother to four winners including useful Zillow

runners.name: Alfey Moon
runners.spotlight: 33-1 and was tenth of the 12 runners on good to soft at Chepstow (6f); limited appeal on that performance.
runners.comment: 33-1 and was tenth of the 12 runners on good to soft at Chepstow (6f)

runners.name: Le Ciel Noble
runners.spotlight: 40-1 and never seen with a chance at Haydock in the race won by stablemate Persian Land.
runners.comment: 40-1 and never seen with a chance at Haydock in the race won by stablemate Persian Land

runners.name: Devious Devan
runners.spotlight: Hurled in on what he achieved as a 3yo and belatedly returned to form when a length winner at Wolverhampton (8.6f; backed into 9-2); raised just 3lb and he has form here.
runners.comment: Back to form when winning on the AW and he had fallen a long way in the weights

runners.name: Toralou
runners.spotlight: Much better record on turf than AW, though one she failed to enhance at Newbury 20 days ago; that was underwhelming.
runners.comment: Much better record on turf than AW, though one she failed to enhance at Newbury

runners.name: Sixfiveseven
runners.spotlight: Newmarket winner in late 2024 (1m, good; fine on soft); second run back from a long absence was better than the first and he might progress again; realistic mark.
runners.comment: Second run back from a long absence was better than the first and he might progress again

runners.name: Thiscouldbefun
runners.spotlight: Two 1m wins last year, on the AW and good to firm (handles soft); off since December but could have a big run in her with Ashley Lewis taking off 5lb.
runners.comment: Dual winner at 1m and could have a big run in her with Ashley Lewis taking off 5lb
runners.owner: Mrs Wendy Jarrett & J S Moore

runners.name: Mereside Madness
runners.spotlight: A four-time winner but has been regressing for a while and no effort of note for current yard.
runners.comment: Four wins but has been regressing for a while and no effort of note for current yard

runners.name: Grey Soul
runners.spotlight: Signed off for Roger Varian with a victory at Newcastle (1m AW) in first-time blinkers; shaped okay on yard debut when in no headgear at Kempton (1m) and could be a factor now tried in cheekpieces.
runners.comment: A winner in blinkers; shaped okay on recent yard debut and now tries cheekpieces

runners.name: Nakaaha
runners.spotlight: Won over 1m at Newbury and here last summer; up and down this season and the recent rain was probably no help.
runners.comment: Course winner; up and down this season and the recent rain was probably no help

runners.name: Eagle Day
runners.spotlight: His 8.6f win at Wolverhampton came out of the blue and he then disappointed at Nottingham; seems far more effective on the AW; tries an eyeshield.
runners.comment: Eyeshield fitted after a poor run; seems far more effective on the AW

runners.name: Auburn Avenue
runners.spotlight: Left Ireland for 15,000euros and a 15-race maiden, though very nearly put that right on the AW (1m) in October; has turf form as well; could be interesting on stable debut should market vibes be at all posititive.
runners.comment: Left Ireland for 15,000euros and a 15-race maiden, but did have placed form there

runners.name: Orchard
runners.spotlight: Sole win and best form has been on the AW, and it's nearly a year since he finished last in a Newbury handicap (1m); perhaps best watched.
runners.comment: Sole win and best form has been on the AW; absent for nearly a year

runners.name: Sea The Light
runners.spotlight: Debut winner who hasn't offered a great deal in four handicaps over longer distances, latterly in cheekpieces (reapplied) and blinkers; bit risky.
runners.comment: Debut winner who hasn't offered a great deal in four handicaps over longer distances

runners.name: Kelly Burn
runners.spotlight: Has improved for handicaps and edged home in a small field at Wolverhampton (1m4f AW); only 2lb higher now but in what looks a better race.
runners.comment: Has improved for handicaps and edged home in a small field at Wolverhampton; up 2lb

runners.name: Salkadan
runners.spotlight: Nearly won his first handicap and went one better in the second, albeit narrowly over a similar trip at Haydock (good); the runner-up was disappointing last weekend but this 3yo only went up 2lb and looks progressive.
runners.comment: Only 2lb higher than for narrow Haydock win and this 3yo looks progressive

runners.name: Reem Rak
runners.spotlight: The form of her second at Newcastle last season reads well (7f AW) and she couldn't have given her true running when made favourite for handicap debut in April (1m); could be given another chance and she's bred for this far.
runners.comment: One run reads well and she couldn't have given her true running on handicap debut

runners.name: Mick The Hat
runners.spotlight: Beaten at least 12l in three qualifying races (1m-8.6f) but now goes handicapping at a modest level and over a longer trip; the market could tell a tale.
runners.comment: Poor so far but now goes handicapping at a modest level and over a longer trip

runners.name: Eze Sur Mer
runners.spotlight: Not that dangerous in her first three races and again when 33-1 for handicap debut at Redcar (1m2f); wouldn't be the obvious answer.
runners.comment: Not that dangerous in her first three races and again when 33-1 for handicap debut

runners.name: Aravalli
runners.spotlight: Showed more on third and final run in Irish maidens, finishing fourth of ten at Cork (1m2f, soft); left Jessica Harrington for 22,000gns; likely best watched unless backed.
runners.comment: Showed more on final run in Irish maidens, finishing fourth of ten; sold for 22,000gns

runners.name: Al's River
runners.spotlight: Improved in last season's novice races at about 1m but it's been a low-key start in handicaps, at 1m2f and 1m3f; trainer turns to blinkers for some inspiration.
runners.comment: Low-key start in handicaps and trainer turns to blinkers for some inspiration

runners.name: Brave Leader
runners.spotlight: Steady improvement last year and appeared to perform below expectations on his handicap debut at Doncaster (1m2f, good to firm; 9-4 favourite); interest to see if backers return.
runners.comment: Appeared to perform below expectations on his handicap debut at Doncaster

runners.name: Willow Wood
runners.spotlight: 200,000gns 2yo who is bred to be better than this mark but she was beaten about 6l in her first handicap over 1m2f on the AW; has to improve on that effort but it's still relatively early days.
runners.comment: Bred to be better than this mark but she was beaten about 6l in her first handicap

runners.name: Buck Barrow
runners.spotlight: Sole win came in 1m2f handicap here last June on good to soft; below par this season but the recent rain heightens interest.
runners.comment: Course winner; below par this season but the recent rain heightens interest

runners.name: Hedonista
runners.spotlight: Dual winner but 0-11 for current yard and essentially regressive; off since January.
runners.comment: Dual winner but 0-11 for current yard and essentially regressive; off since January

runners.name: Change Of Fortune
runners.spotlight: Strike-rate of 1-36 but not had many chances at 1m4f over which he was beaten 3l in a classified race on the AW last time; possible.
runners.comment: Not had many chances at 1m4f over which he was beaten 3l in a classified race latest

runners.name: Adrian
runners.spotlight: A regressive 9yo who last won in 2021, a 1m4f Group 3 in Germany; well beaten after a break and probably wants 2m ideally.
runners.comment: Regressive 9yo; well beaten after a break and probably wants 2m ideally

runners.name: Lamlash
runners.spotlight: Pulled up in one hurdle start and has yet to beat a rival on the Flat, including a handicap.
runners.comment: Pulled up in one hurdle race and has yet to beat a rival on the Flat, including a handicap
runners.owner: H Maye And The Joe Tickle Racing Club

runners.name: Oceanides
runners.spotlight: Poor form in a bumper and three runs on the AW at up to 9.4f; has a BHA mark of 23.
runners.comment: Poor form in a bumper and three runs on the AW at up to 9.4f; has a BHA mark of 23

runners.name: Ghost Story
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced and her two wins have been over 1m2f on good to soft and soft; perhaps a tad high in the weights but her AW reappearance was respectable and this scenario suits her better.
runners.comment: High in the weights but AW reappearance was respectable and this scenario suits her better

runners.name: Risen Again
runners.spotlight: A fair 1m2f winner at his best but yet to run a race of note since leaving Alice Haynes.
runners.comment: A fair 1m2f winner at his best but yet to run a race of note since leaving Alice Haynes

runners.name: Premier
runners.spotlight: Both wins, including one over hurdles, came in a hood, which was absent when well beaten on the AW last month and again here; others preferred.
runners.comment: Both wins, including one over hurdles, came in a hood; looks opposable

runners.name: Crackergee
runners.spotlight: 11-race maiden having only his third run on turf; yet to find his best form this season and the headgear tried last time is dispensed with.
runners.comment: 11-race maiden having only his third run on turf; yet to find his best form this season

runners.name: Zambezi Magic
runners.spotlight: Ran okay after a break on the AW and he loves it here, especially when the mud is flying; the recent rain has helped his cause no end.
runners.comment: Ran okay after a break on the AW and he loves it here, especially when the mud is flying

runners.name: Mighty Quiet
runners.spotlight: Effective on good and heavy; quiet comeback run and she can be inconsistent, but she's a dual winner on a fair mark and very capable at this level.
runners.comment: She's a dual winner on a fair mark and very capable at this level

runners.name: Navy Seal
runners.spotlight: Possesses winning form at this track; mostly consistent recently; comfortably beaten despite finishing third over 1m4f at Keeneland (allowance Grade 2) 47 days ago, effort boosted by Fleetfoot winning since; this might be easier; stable is ticking along nicely; capable of going well.
runners.comment: Needs consideration; successful twice in his last four starts.

runners.name: Corruption
runners.spotlight: Third over 1m1f at Churchill Downs in the Turf Classic when dropped in distance five weeks ago; often been there or thereabouts lately; ran well in a Grade 3 in March; an easier task than his previous race; warrants respect.
runners.comment: Must be high on list; third in the Turf Classic five weeks ago.

runners.name: Miztertonic
runners.spotlight: Not beaten far over 1m3f at Belmont At The Big A (allowance optional claimer) last month; possesses winning form at this track; admirably consistent; now tried over a longer trip; class hike now; into allowance company.
runners.comment: Beaten three-quarters of a length 28 days ago; admirably consistent.

runners.name: Concord Green
runners.spotlight: Track has proven suitable before; quick away to lead from a wide draw at Keeneland 49 days ago when beaten 1 1/2l to finish second in an allowance race; steps up to allowance company; takes a step into the unknown as he moves up in distance.
runners.comment: Gave good account 49 days ago; possesses winning form at this track.

runners.name: Flashiest
runners.spotlight: Cracking effort in defeat when dropped in distance last month at Santa Anita in a Grade 2; has maintained his consistent level of form; longer trip presents a different challenge.
runners.comment: Just denied last month; has been fairly consistent lately.
runners.owner: Wachtel Stable, Gary Barber & Nicolas G Drion

runners.name: Tawny Port
runners.spotlight: Has won at this course; was held in fourth in an allowance Grade 2 47 days ago at Keeneland splitting Navy Seal and Fleetfoot, form boosted by Fleetfoot; could have less on his plate in this; has to be taken seriously as shown some ability on occasion and he has knocked on the door a number of times.
runners.comment: He should go close as boasts the best speed figure posted by this field in the last 12 months.

runners.name: Parchment Party
runners.spotlight: Unbeaten at this course after two runs and continued his winning streak when last seen; got the job done in a Listed race by just over 1l at Churchill Downs (sloppy track) five weeks ago; moves up into an allowance race; longer trip presents a different challenge.
runners.comment: Narrow winner five weeks ago; unbeaten at this course after two runs.

runners.name: Fleetfoot
runners.spotlight: Got across to lead early from a wide draw in an allowance race at Fair Hill last week, scoring by 3l; career record shows his ability to win (wins-runs record of 6-20); up in class to allowance company.
runners.comment: Scored by 3l last Monday; successful six times from 20 career runs.

runners.name: Worthington
runners.spotlight: No threat to winner, Fleetfoot, at Far Hills in October (finished third) upped to this trip in a; looking for the third success of his career; class hike now; into allowance company; back from an absence here.
runners.comment: Third beaten 11l in October; searching for his third career win.
